message.content:
You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_61.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 61: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[61]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_61.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 61: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""I-deepfix-001 (#1344) item 2 — winner-firing fail-closed gate (relevance layer).
2	
3	WHY THIS EXISTS
4	---------------
5	Two winners-only PAID runs (FRONT=drb_72, MIDDLE=drb_76) burned GPU + tokens
6	while the relevance-layer WINNERS were structurally DARK: the W6 embedder / B4
7	semantic scorer fell back to the legacy lexical cut every round, and the W5
8	content-relevance reranker logged ``reranker_device=unavailable`` and reverted to
9	full weight for every passage. The deliverable would have been labeled
10	"winners-only" while three of the headline winners never fired. A 7-agent
11	line-by-line forensic read found only 4 of 14 winners fired.
12	
13	WHAT THIS MODULE IS
14	-------------------
15	A PURE, offline-testable decision function. It reads the ALREADY-DETERMINED
16	structural state of the relevance-layer winners at the post-retrieval /
17	pre-generation seam and returns a verdict: which REQUESTED winners are
18	structurally dark, and whether the run must ABORT before the expensive
19	generation. It performs ZERO model loads, ZERO network, ZERO GPU work — it only
20	inspects state the retrieval phase already produced.
21	
22	THE DISTINCTION (so this is NOT a faithfulness hold — CLAUDE.md §-1.3)
23	---------------------------------------------------------------------
24	This is a CONFIG / WIRING firing-gate: a relevance-layer WINNER is dark =>
25	the run is NOT actually running winners-only => abort BEFORE producing a
26	falsely-labeled "winners-only" deliverable. It is NOT a faithfulness hold on
27	rendered claims. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / 4-role / D8 / NLI /
28	span-grounding) is UNTOUCHED and still always releases + labels per §-1.3. A
29	"structural dark" here means the model could not be IMPORTED / LOADED at all
30	(the wiring is wrong) — never a single transient encode exception, and never a
31	weight/down-weight decision on a source (no source is ever dropped here).
32	
33	WHAT COUNTS AS STRUCTURAL-DARK (per the Codex-approved fix brief item 2)
34	-----------------------------------------------------------------------
35	A winner is dark ONLY when it was REQUESTED (its slate flag is ON) AND its
36	structural state proves a load/import failure (NOT a transient):
37	
38	  * W6 embedder / B4 semantic scorer: the cached embedder handle is the ``False``
39	    sentinel (``evidence_selector._SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE is False`` == "load
40	    attempted, unavailable") OR the selection telemetry shows the semantic scorer
41	    was REQUESTED but fell back to the lexical scorer.
42	  * W5 content-relevance reranker: ``retrieval.content_relevance['reranker_device']
43	    == 'unavailable'`` (set by content_relevance_judge when the reranker LOAD
44	    raised → full weight for all passages).
45	  * W7 selection reranker: a caller-supplied ``w7_load_failed`` flag (the selection
46	    reranker fires AFTER this seam, so its structural-dark state is supplied by the
47	    caller when known; absent => not yet determined => not tripped here).
48	
49	A CPU fallback (``used_cpu_fallback``) is a DISCLOSED degrade, NOT a structural
50	dark — the winner DID fire, just slower. It does NOT trip this gate (§-1.3:
51	disclosed down-weight, never an abort on a working-but-slow path).
52	"""
53	
54	from __future__ import annotations
55	
56	import os
57	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
58	from typing import Any
59	
60	
61	# Truthy env values shared with the winner flags (mirrors the per-winner enabled
62	# checks in content_relevance_judge.content_relevance_enabled /
63	# evidence_selector._reranker_selection_enabled — duplicated here as a pure,
64	# import-free check so this module never pulls the heavy retrieval chain).
65	_ON_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on"})
66	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", ""})
67	
68	
69	def _env_on(name: str, default: str) -> bool:
70	    """True iff env var ``name`` (defaulting to ``default``) is an ON value."""
71	    return os.getenv(name, default).strip().lower() in _ON_VALUES
72	
73	
74	def _w6_embedder_requested() -> bool:
75	    """W6 embedder / B4 semantic scorer requested.
76	
77	    The B4 semantic relevance scorer is requested when the redesign credibility
78	    pass is ON (PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN, default 'on') AND a semantic
79	    embedder model is configured (PG_EMBEDDER_MODEL set to a non-empty value).
80	    The Gate-B slate sets PG_EMBEDDER_MODEL=qwen3, so on the deepfix winners path
81	    this is True; on the default/legacy path PG_EMBEDDER_MODEL is unset => False
82	    => the gate never trips (byte-identical OFF).
83	    """
84	    if not _env_on("PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN", "on"):
85	        return False
86	    return bool(os.getenv("PG_EMBEDDER_MODEL", "").strip())
87	
88	
89	def _w5_content_relevance_requested() -> bool:
90	    """W5 content-relevance reranker requested (PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE, default ON)."""
91	    return _env_on("PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE", "1")
92	
93	
94	def _w7_reranker_requested() -> bool:
95	    """W7 selection reranker requested.
96	
97	    The Gate-B slate sets ``PG_RERANKER_MODEL=qwen3`` (a MODEL NAME, not a boolean
98	    ON value), so the prior ``in _ON_VALUES`` check wrongly read it as NOT-requested
99	    and W7 was never hard-gated. Mirror ``_w6_embedder_requested``'s
100	    ``bool(os.getenv(...).strip())`` style: requested iff PG_RERANKER_MODEL is a
101	    NON-EMPTY value — EXCEPT the explicit off-values (``0``/``false``/``no``/``off``/
102	    ``""``) which mean not-requested (byte-identical OFF on the default/legacy path
103	    where PG_RERANKER_MODEL is unset). This matches the per-winner enabled check in
104	    ``evidence_selector._reranker_selection_enabled`` (any model name turns W7 on).
105	    """
106	    value = os.environ.get("PG_RERANKER_MODEL", "").strip().lower()
107	    return bool(value) and value not in _OFF_VALUES
108	
109	
110	@dataclass
111	class WinnerFiringVerdict:
112	    """The pure gate verdict. ``abort`` True => the run MUST stop before generation."""
113	
114	    abort: bool = False
115	    dark_winners: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
116	    diagnostics: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
117	    # Per-winner state, for the disclosed manifest field (never a drop list).
118	    winners_checked: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
119	
120	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
121	        return {
122	            "abort": self.abort,
123	            "dark_winners": list(self.dark_winners),
124	            "diagnostics": list(self.diagnostics),
125	            "winners_checked": dict(self.winners_checked),
126	        }
127	
128	
129	def evaluate_winner_firing(
130	    *,
131	    content_relevance: dict[str, Any] | None,
132	    embedder_cache_sentinel: Any,
133	    w6_requested: bool | None = None,
134	    w5_requested: bool | None = None,
135	    w7_requested: bool | None = None,
136	    semantic_fell_back: bool = False,
137	    w7_load_failed: bool | None = None,
138	) -> WinnerFiringVerdict:
139	    """Decide whether a relevance-layer winner is STRUCTURALLY dark.
140	
141	    PURE: no IO, no model load, no network. All inputs are already-determined
142	    state captured by the retrieval phase.
143	
144	    Args:
145	      content_relevance: ``retrieval.content_relevance`` (the W5 / W2 report
146	        ``to_dict()``), or None when W5 was OFF / never produced a report. The
147	        structural-dark signal is ``reranker_device == 'unavailable'``.
148	      embedder_cache_sentinel: ``evidence_selector._SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE``.
149	        The ``False`` sentinel == "embedder load attempted and FAILED" (structural
150	        dark for W6). ``None`` == not yet tried (NOT dark). A truthy handle ==
151	        loaded fine (NOT dark).
152	      w6_requested / w5_requested / w7_requested: override the env-derived
153	        "was this winner requested" checks (used by tests; None => read env).
154	      semantic_fell_back: True iff the selection telemetry recorded the semantic
155	        scorer was REQUESTED but fell back to lexical (a second W6/B4 dark signal,
156	        independent of the cache sentinel).
157	      w7_load_failed: True iff the caller KNOWS the W7 selection reranker failed to
158	        load structurally. None => W7 load state not yet determined at this seam =>
159	        W7 is not tripped here (it fires after generation begins; its own loud
160	        fallback + the identity log tag cover the post-seam case).
161	
162	    Returns a ``WinnerFiringVerdict``. ``abort`` is True iff at least one REQUESTED
163	    winner is structurally dark.
164	    """
165	    w6_on = _w6_embedder_requested() if w6_requested is None else w6_requested
166	    w5_on = _w5_content_relevance_requested() if w5_requested is None else w5_requested
167	    w7_on = _w7_reranker_requested() if w7_requested is None else w7_requested
168	
169	    verdict = WinnerFiringVerdict()
170	
171	    # ── W6 embedder / B4 semantic scorer ─────────────────────────────────────
172	    if w6_on:
173	        # `False` sentinel = load attempted and failed (structural). `None` = not
174	        # yet tried (not dark). Truthy handle = loaded (not dark).
175	        cache_dark = embedder_cache_sentinel is False
176	        if cache_dark or semantic_fell_back:
177	            verdict.dark_winners.append("W6_embedder")
178	            verdict.winners_checked["W6_embedder"] = "dark"
179	            reason = (
180	                "cached embedder handle is the False sentinel (load attempted, unavailable)"
181	                if cache_dark
182	                else "semantic scorer requested but fell back LOUDLY to the lexical cut"
183	            )
184	            verdict.diagnostics.append(
185	                f"W6 embedder / B4 semantic scorer STRUCTURALLY DARK: {reason}. "
186	                "The relevance-layer winner did not fire — the run is NOT winners-only."
187	            )
188	        else:
189	            verdict.winners_checked["W6_embedder"] = "fired_or_pending"
190	    else:
191	        verdict.winners_checked["W6_embedder"] = "not_requested"
192	
193	    # ── W5 content-relevance reranker ────────────────────────────────────────
194	    if w5_on:
195	        cr = content_relevance or {}
196	        device = str(cr.get("reranker_device", "") or "").strip().lower()
197	        if device == "unavailable":
198	            verdict.dark_winners.append("W5_content_relevance")
199	            verdict.winners_checked["W5_content_relevance"] = "dark"
200	            verdict.diagnostics.append(
201	                "W5 content-relevance reranker STRUCTURALLY DARK: reranker_device="
202	                "'unavailable' (load failed → full weight for all passages). The "
203	                "relevance-layer winner did not fire — the run is NOT winners-only."
204	            )
205	        elif content_relevance is None:
206	            # Requested but produced NO report at all (the judge never ran). This is
207	            # a structural miss for a force-ON winner.
208	            verdict.dark_winners.append("W5_content_relevance")
209	            verdict.winners_checked["W5_content_relevance"] = "dark"
210	            verdict.diagnostics.append(
211	                "W5 content-relevance reranker REQUESTED (PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE "
212	                "on) but produced NO telemetry report — the judge never ran. The "
213	                "relevance-layer winner did not fire — the run is NOT winners-only."
214	            )
215	        else:
216	            verdict.winners_checked["W5_content_relevance"] = (
217	                "cpu_fallback_disclosed" if cr.get("used_cpu_fallback") else "fired"
218	            )
219	    else:
220	        verdict.winners_checked["W5_content_relevance"] = "not_requested"
221	
222	    # ── W7 selection reranker ────────────────────────────────────────────────
223	    if w7_on:
224	        if w7_load_failed is True:
225	            verdict.dark_winners.append("W7_reranker")
226	            verdict.winners_checked["W7_reranker"] = "dark"
227	            verdict.diagnostics.append(
228	                "W7 selection reranker (Qwen3-Reranker-4B) STRUCTURALLY DARK: the "
229	                "model failed to load. The relevance-layer winner did not fire — the "
230	                "run is NOT winners-only."
231	            )
232	        elif w7_load_failed is None:
233	            # The selection reranker fires AFTER this seam; its load state is not
234	            # known here. Record as pending — NOT a trip.
235	            verdict.winners_checked["W7_reranker"] = "pending_post_seam"
236	        else:
237	            verdict.winners_checked["W7_reranker"] = "fired"
238	    else:
239	        verdict.winners_checked["W7_reranker"] = "not_requested"
240	
241	    verdict.abort = bool(verdict.dark_winners)
242	    return verdict
243

1	"""Per-role output contracts for the POLARIS 4-role architecture (I-meta-002 sub-PR-2).
2	
3	Pure parsers/normalizers over a raw role-output string + minimal context, returning a
4	typed result. NO network, NO spend — each role is exercised here ONLY via fixtures.
5	
6	Roles (operator-LOCKED, NOT consultable):
7	- Generator: deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro
8	- Mirror:    cohere/command-a-plus      -> mirror_contract
9	- Sentinel:  ibm-granite/granite-guardian-4.1-8b -> sentinel_contract (fail-CLOSED)
10	- Judge:     moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 (benchmark; sovereign lock qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b) -> judge_contract (5-enum reuse, hard-fail)
11	
12	This is the contract layer only. Adapter wiring is sub-PR-4; spend is sub-PR-5; the
13	release-gate wiring of the source_missing split is sub-PR-3.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17

content.text: This first file has heavy naming - "winner_firing_gate", W5/W6/W7 - these are project-specific domain terms tied to an internal doc (Codex-approved fix brief), somewhat cryptic but consistently used. Let's continue with the rest of the files.

content.content:
1	"""I-wire-007 (#1321) — ADAPTIVE AIMD concurrency controller for the 4-role D8 verifier transport.
2	
3	THE PROBLEM (I-wire-006 #1320 root cause): the 4-role D8 verify settles only a trickle of claims
4	because the per-role concurrency is a FIXED static cap (`PG_FOUR_ROLE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY=4`,
5	`PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_WORKERS=6`). A fixed cap cannot ride the live OpenRouter ceiling: too LOW and
6	the run is needlessly slow; too HIGH and it triggers a 429 storm (judge / qwen) or floods the slow
7	minimax host (sentinel) into the trickle-200 that the per-call deadline then force-closes. The
8	bottleneck is provider-chain throughput, NOT the verdict logic.
9	
10	THE FIX — TCP-congestion AIMD (Additive-Increase / Multiplicative-Decrease). The exact, decades-proven
11	algorithm a TCP sender uses to find and ride a link's capacity without a known bandwidth: probe UP
12	slowly (add 1 to the in-flight limit after a clean window), back DOWN hard (halve) the instant a
13	congestion signal (a 429 / a force-close timeout) appears. It AUTO-FINDS the ceiling and stays just
14	under it, adapting as the provider's capacity shifts mid-run. Bounded to [min, max] so it can never
15	collapse to zero or run away.
16	
17	FAITHFULNESS IS FROZEN (I-faith-001 C0). This controller is the THROUGHPUT/transport layer ONLY: it
18	governs ONLY HOW MANY role POSTs run concurrently, never which claim passes / holds. A slow / dropped
19	/ timed-out claim still hits the SAME fail-closed `RoleTransportError` -> the 4-role gate HOLDS /
20	UNGROUNDED. `_compose_final_verdict`, the Sentinel downgrade ladder, and the entailment decision are
21	UNTOUCHED. Lowering the in-flight limit only DELAYS work; it never changes a verdict.
22	
23	PER-ROLE, not global (I-wire-006 review): a 429 is the qwen JUDGE rate limit; a force-close is the
24	minimax SENTINEL slow-host timeout. A single global controller would let a sentinel force-close
25	spuriously throttle the judge (and vice-versa). Each role gets its OWN controller fed by its OWN
26	signal.
27	
28	LAW VI: every knob is env-driven (named, no magic numbers), read at construction. Default ON, but a
29	disabled controller (or OFF via `PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_CONCURRENCY=0`) is byte-equivalent to the
30	prior static `threading.BoundedSemaphore` cap so the OFF path can never start worse than baseline.
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	# Standard library
36	import os
37	import threading
38	
39	
40	# === LAW VI: env knob names (no magic numbers; documented defaults) ============================
41	_ENABLE_ENV = "PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_CONCURRENCY"
42	_ENABLE_DEFAULT = "1"  # default ON (concurrency-only; OFF is byte-identical to the static cap).
43	
44	# Per-role knob SUFFIXES; the resolved env var is f"PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_{ROLE}_{SUFFIX}".
45	# A role-specific override ALWAYS wins; otherwise the generic PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_<SUFFIX> is read;
46	# otherwise the coded default below. The defaults are deliberately conservative — the MIN equals the
47	# proven static baseline so an adaptive run can never begin below the cap it replaces.
48	_MIN_SUFFIX = "MIN"
49	_MAX_SUFFIX = "MAX"
50	_STEP_SUFFIX = "STEP"
51	_BACKOFF_SUFFIX = "BACKOFF"
52	_PROBE_WINDOW_SUFFIX = "PROBE_WINDOW"
53	
54	# Coded per-role defaults. MIN == the historical static cap for that role (judge 4, sentinel was
55	# unbounded -> a conservative 4 floor when adaptive is ON), MAX rides the ceiling, STEP=1 (additive
56	# +1 per clean window), BACKOFF=0.5 (halve on a congestion signal), PROBE_WINDOW=8 (clean successes
57	# before one additive step — long enough that a single lucky call does not ramp into a storm).
58	_ROLE_DEFAULTS: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {
59	    "judge": {
60	        _MIN_SUFFIX: "4",      # == PG_FOUR_ROLE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY historical default.
61	        _MAX_SUFFIX: "16",
62	        _STEP_SUFFIX: "1",
63	        _BACKOFF_SUFFIX: "0.5",
64	        _PROBE_WINDOW_SUFFIX: "8",
65	    },
66	    "sentinel": {
67	        _MIN_SUFFIX: "4",      # sentinel was unbounded; a small adaptive floor rides up from here.
68	        _MAX_SUFFIX: "12",
69	        _STEP_SUFFIX: "1",
70	        _BACKOFF_SUFFIX: "0.5",
71	        _PROBE_WINDOW_SUFFIX: "8",
72	    },
73	}
74	
75	
76	def adaptive_concurrency_enabled() -> bool:
77	    """Whether the AIMD controller governs per-role concurrency (default ON).
78	
79	    OFF (`0`/`false`/`no`/`off`) reverts every role to its prior STATIC `BoundedSemaphore` cap —
80	    byte-equivalent to pre-#1321. Read at controller-construction time.
81	    """
82	    return os.getenv(_ENABLE_ENV, _ENABLE_DEFAULT).strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
83	
84	
85	def _resolve_int(role: str, suffix: str) -> int:
86	    """Resolve a per-role integer knob: role-specific env > generic env > coded default. Min 1."""
87	    role_env = f"PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_{role.upper()}_{suffix}"
88	    generic_env = f"PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_{suffix}"
89	    default = _ROLE_DEFAULTS.get(role, {}).get(suffix, "1")
90	    raw = os.getenv(role_env) or os.getenv(generic_env) or default
91	    try:
92	        return max(1, int(raw))
93	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
94	        return max(1, int(default))
95	
96	
97	def _resolve_float(role: str, suffix: str) -> float:
98	    """Resolve a per-role float knob (the BACKOFF multiplier): role-specific > generic > default."""
99	    role_env = f"PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_{role.upper()}_{suffix}"
100	    generic_env = f"PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_{suffix}"
101	    default = _ROLE_DEFAULTS.get(role, {}).get(suffix, "0.5")
102	    raw = os.getenv(role_env) or os.getenv(generic_env) or default
103	    try:
104	        value = float(raw)
105	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
106	        value = float(default)
107	    # Clamp into (0, 1): a multiplicative DECREASE must shrink (>=1 would never back off) and stay
108	    # above 0 (0 would collapse the window to the floor in one hit, defeating the gentle AIMD ride).
109	    if not (0.0 < value < 1.0):
110	        value = float(default)
111	    return value
112	
113	
114	class AdaptiveConcurrencyController:
115	    """A thread-safe DYNAMIC-limit concurrency gate driven by TCP-congestion AIMD.
116	
117	    Unlike `threading.BoundedSemaphore` (which CANNOT change size mid-run — the documented reason the
118	    static judge/sentinel semaphores only ever loosen), this gate's limit both GROWS and SHRINKS at
119	    runtime under a single `threading.Condition`:
120	
121	      * `acquire()` blocks while `in_flight >= limit`, then increments `in_flight`.
122	      * `release()` decrements `in_flight` and notifies one waiter.
123	      * `on_success()` / `on_throttle()` / `on_timeout()` mutate `limit` per the AIMD rules and
124	        notify waiters when the limit grows (so a blocked acquirer can proceed immediately).
125	
126	    The AIMD math (`on_*`) is PURE w.r.t. the lock-protected `_limit` / `_clean_streak` state and is
127	    unit-tested directly (no threads, no network): additive +`step` after `probe_window` clean
128	    successes, multiplicative *`backoff` on a throttle/timeout, clamped to `[min_limit, max_limit]`.
129	
130	    FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: governs ONLY concurrency. A throttled limit delays work; it never changes a
131	    verdict. The role transport's existing fail-closed `RoleTransportError` path is untouched.
132	    """
133	
134	    def __init__(
135	        self,
136	        *,
137	        min_limit: int,
138	        max_limit: int,
139	        step: int = 1,
140	        backoff: float = 0.5,
141	        probe_window: int = 8,
142	        start_limit: int | None = None,
143	    ) -> None:
144	        min_limit = max(1, int(min_limit))
145	        max_limit = max(min_limit, int(max_limit))
146	        self._min_limit = min_limit
147	        self._max_limit = max_limit
148	        self._step = max(1, int(step))
149	        # backoff clamped into (0,1): a decrease must shrink and never reach 0.
150	        self._backoff = backoff if 0.0 < backoff < 1.0 else 0.5
151	        self._probe_window = max(1, int(probe_window))
152	        # Start at the FLOOR (TCP slow-start analogue): probe UP from the known-safe min, never
153	        # begin at the max and provoke an immediate storm.
154	        start = self._min_limit if start_limit is None else int(start_limit)
155	        self._limit = min(self._max_limit, max(self._min_limit, start))
156	        self._in_flight = 0
157	        self._clean_streak = 0
158	        # I-wire-010 (#1324): one-way TEARDOWN latch. While False (the entire normal run) the gate is
159	        # byte-identical to pre-#1324; `release_all()` sets it at process teardown so every acquirer
160	        # blocked in `acquire()`'s `self._cond.wait()` wakes and stops gating (no worker can hang on
161	        # this Condition while the interpreter joins lingering pool threads at exit).
162	        self._shutdown = False
163	        self._cond = threading.Condition()
164	
165	    # --- the gate ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
166	    def acquire(self) -> None:
167	        """Block until an in-flight slot is free under the CURRENT (possibly shrunk) limit, then take it.
168	
169	        I-wire-010 (#1324): the wait also breaks when the `_shutdown` teardown latch is set, so a
170	        blocked acquirer can never hang on this Condition during process teardown. The `and not
171	        self._shutdown` guard is byte-identical to pre-#1324 for the whole normal run (the latch is
172	        only ever set by `release_all()` at exit)."""
173	        with self._cond:
174	            while self._in_flight >= self._limit and not self._shutdown:
175	                self._cond.wait()
176	            self._in_flight += 1
177	
178	    def release(self) -> None:
179	        """Free this caller's in-flight slot and wake one waiter."""
180	        with self._cond:
181	            if self._in_flight > 0:
182	                self._in_flight -= 1
183	            self._cond.notify()
184	
185	    def release_all(self) -> None:
186	        """TEARDOWN ONLY (I-wire-010 #1324): wake EVERY acquirer blocked in ``acquire()``'s
187	        ``self._cond.wait()`` so no worker thread can hang on this Condition at process exit.
188	
189	        Sets the one-way ``_shutdown`` latch (so a re-looping or freshly-arriving acquirer also stops
190	        gating) and ``notify_all()``. A woken acquirer falls through the ``acquire`` loop and takes its
191	        slot UNGATED — correct because teardown only runs once the run is already ending, so granting
192	        concurrency can no longer change any verdict (FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: this is the
193	        transport/concurrency layer, never the decision). Idempotent; never raises."""
194	        with self._cond:
195	            self._shutdown = True
196	            self._cond.notify_all()
197	
198	    # --- AIMD signals (pure math under the lock; unit-tested directly) ----------------------------
199	    def on_success(self) -> None:
200	        """Additive-increase: after `probe_window` consecutive clean successes, raise the limit by
201	        `step` (clamped to `max_limit`) and wake waiters so the new headroom is used immediately."""
202	        with self._cond:
203	            self._clean_streak += 1
204	            if self._clean_streak >= self._probe_window and self._limit < self._max_limit:
205	                self._limit = min(self._max_limit, self._limit + self._step)
206	                self._clean_streak = 0
207	                self._cond.notify_all()
208	
209	    def on_throttle(self) -> None:
210	        """Multiplicative-decrease on a 429/503 (rate-limit) signal: halve the limit (clamped to
211	        `min_limit`) and reset the clean streak. The shrink takes effect on the NEXT acquire (an
212	        in-flight overage drains naturally as slots release — never an over-release of the gate)."""
213	        self._multiplicative_decrease()
214	
215	    def on_timeout(self) -> None:
216	        """Multiplicative-decrease on a force-close / total-deadline timeout (a congestion signal for
217	        the slow-host sentinel leg) — identical AIMD back-off to `on_throttle`."""
218	        self._multiplicative_decrease()
219	
220	    def _multiplicative_decrease(self) -> None:
221	        with self._cond:
222	            self._limit = max(self._min_limit, int(self._limit * self._backoff))
223	            self._clean_streak = 0
224	            # No notify: the limit only SHRANK, so no blocked acquirer can newly proceed.
225	
226	    # --- introspection (telemetry / tests; never gates) -------------------------------------------
227	    @property
228	    def limit(self) -> int:
229	        with self._cond:
230	            return self._limit
231	
232	    @property
233	    def in_flight(self) -> int:
234	        with self._cond:
235	            return self._in_flight
236	
237	    @property
238	    def bounds(self) -> tuple[int, int]:
239	        return (self._min_limit, self._max_limit)
240	
241	
242	def build_role_controller(role: str) -> AdaptiveConcurrencyController:
243	    """Construct the AIMD controller for `role` from its env-resolved knobs (LAW VI).
244	
245	    The MIN floor equals the role's proven static cap, so an adaptive run can never start below the
246	    baseline it replaces. Read at construction; the transport builds one controller per role lazily.
247	    """
248	    min_limit = _resolve_int(role, _MIN_SUFFIX)
249	    max_limit = _resolve_int(role, _MAX_SUFFIX)
250	    step = _resolve_int(role, _STEP_SUFFIX)
251	    backoff = _resolve_float(role, _BACKOFF_SUFFIX)
252	    probe_window = _resolve_int(role, _PROBE_WINDOW_SUFFIX)
253	    return AdaptiveConcurrencyController(
254	        min_limit=min_limit,
255	        max_limit=max_limit,
256	        step=step,
257	        backoff=backoff,
258	        probe_window=probe_window,
259	    )
260

1	"""Contract-field label-prefix normalizer (I-deepfix-001 tail-B1 #1344, finding #10). LEAF, PURE.
2	
3	THE BUG (drb_72, claim 01-002 vs 01-007): two claims carry the IDENTICAL verbatim Acemoglu-Restrepo
4	robots figure, but one carries a LEAKED per-trial-subsection CONTRACT-FIELD LABEL prefix — the model
5	echoed the M50 subsection prompt's element label ("Effect estimate WITH uncertainty (CI, SD, or
6	p-value)") into the sentence as ``Effect estimate with uncertainty: One more robot per thousand
7	workers reduces ...``. Because the downstream ``_normalize_sentence`` only lowercases + collapses
8	whitespace, the label survives into (a) the deterministic ``claim_id`` hash (so the twin gets a
9	DIFFERENT id) and (b) the four-role D8 entailment input (so the two twins can settle to DIVERGENT
10	verdicts: 01-007 VERIFIED, 01-002 UNSUPPORTED). Consolidation/dedup also never collapses them.
11	
12	THE FIX: strip a RECOGNIZED contract-field label that appears as a leading ``LABEL:`` prefix at the
13	START of the claim, BEFORE the claim_id hash and BEFORE the entailment input, and normalize the same
14	prefix out of the four-role dedup key + the fact-dedup exact-duplicate check. Stripping the label
15	recovers the ACTUAL claim (the verbatim figure the source stated) — the SAME text the VERIFIED twin
16	carries — so the twins collapse to one representative + one consistent verdict.
17	
18	FAITHFULNESS-SAFE (never relaxes the ONE hard gate): the stripped text is a NON-CLAIM contract-field
19	label, not evidence. Only a CURATED set of labels is recognized, and ONLY when it sits at the very
20	start of the claim immediately followed by a colon — so legitimate prose that merely contains one of
21	these words is never touched, and NO number, citation, or claim content is ever removed. The residual
22	text still passes the UNCHANGED strict_verify / NLI / provenance / span-grounding engine.
23	"""
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import re
27	
28	# The M50 per-trial-subsection contract's 7 element labels (multi_section_generator.
29	# _M50_SUBSECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT) plus their common natural-language spellings. A leaked label is the
30	# model echoing one of these as a ``LABEL:`` sentence prefix. Curated + anchored (see module note) so
31	# a stray occurrence in real prose is never matched. Longest-first is enforced by the regex build.
32	_CONTRACT_FIELD_LABELS = (
33	    "effect estimate with uncertainty",
34	    "effect estimate",
35	    "primary endpoint",
36	    "primary outcome",
37	    "secondary endpoint",
38	    "baseline characteristics",
39	    "sample size",
40	    "population",
41	    "comparator",
42	    "timepoint",
43	    "safety caveat",
44	    "safety signal",
45	)
46	
47	# Build ONE anchored, case-insensitive alternation, longest label first (so "effect estimate with
48	# uncertainty" wins over "effect estimate"). Matches ``^<ws><LABEL><ws>:<ws>`` only — the leading
49	# ``^`` anchor is what keeps a mid-sentence mention of a label word untouched.
50	_LABEL_ALTERNATION = "|".join(
51	    re.escape(lbl) for lbl in sorted(_CONTRACT_FIELD_LABELS, key=len, reverse=True)
52	)
53	_CONTRACT_FIELD_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(
54	    r"^\s*(?:" + _LABEL_ALTERNATION + r")\s*:\s*",
55	    re.IGNORECASE,
56	)
57	
58	
59	def strip_contract_field_prefix(sentence: str) -> str:
60	    """Remove a leading recognized contract-field ``LABEL:`` prefix from ``sentence``. PURE.
61	
62	    Strips at most ONE such prefix (the labels do not chain in the observed leak). Returns the
63	    input UNCHANGED (byte-identical) when no recognized label prefixes the claim — so a normal
64	    sentence, or a sentence that merely mentions a label word later on, is never altered. Never
65	    removes a number, citation, or claim content: the match is anchored to the start and ends at
66	    the first colon's trailing whitespace."""
67	    if not sentence:
68	        return sentence
69	    return _CONTRACT_FIELD_PREFIX_RE.sub("", sentence, count=1)
70

1	"""S0 safety-floor coverage binder (I-perm-020 #1212) — DEFAULT-OFF, additive credit.
2	
3	PURE FUNCTION, NO NETWORK, NO SPEND. This module credits a required S0 SAFETY category to an
4	ALREADY-VERIFIED claim AFTER per-claim verification (never inside generation), so the D8
5	safety-floor (``release_policy.apply_d8_release_policy``) sees the credited category. It exists to
6	fix the measured drb_76 false hold: a VERIFIED contraindication claim ("not recommended"/"should be
7	avoided" for an immunocompromised population, citing the exact CDC source) carried
8	``s0_categories=[]`` because its text never used the literal token "contraindicated", so D8 coverage
9	stayed < 0.70 and the run safety-floored as ``released_insufficient_safety_evidence`` despite a
10	faithful safety claim.
11	
12	WHAT IT DOES (and just as importantly, what it does NOT):
13	
14	* It ADDS coverage/credit on TOP of verification. It NEVER relaxes a faithfulness gate, NEVER
15	  changes the D8 decision threshold, and NEVER changes the release rule. A category it credits at
16	  build time is STILL gated downstream by the 4-role ``verdict == VERIFIED`` check in
17	  ``release_policy`` (a claim the 4-role rejects earns NO S0 credit) — so this is an additive credit,
18	  not a gate relaxation.
19	
20	* The credit rule mirrors the design ruling in ``docs/drb76_downstream_solutions.md`` (hurdle #2):
21	  ``credit(category) = strict_verify VERIFIED AND assertion is a contraindication DIRECTION (not its
22	  negation) AND semantic_relation maps to the required category AND entity slots bound AND polarity
23	  not ABSENT``. Concretely it reuses the WHOLE I-perm-002 conjunction (NOT contraindication-direction
24	  alone): (a) the claim must cite evidence whose canonical identifier EXACTLY matches the entity's
25	  (``_entity_canonical_match``); AND (b) every non-concept content token (e.g. the population anchor
26	  "immunocompromised") must be literal-exact present; AND (c) the contraindication CONCEPT token must
27	  be satisfied by a high-precision contraindication-DIRECTION phrase; AND (d) the deterministic
28	  negation guard must pass (a negated/inverted/absent phrase earns NO credit). Crediting on direction
29	  WITHOUT the population anchor + canonical evidence match is the over-credit failure this avoids.
30	
31	* DIRECTION OF ERROR (binding, §-1.1): over-crediting a contraindication is LETHAL (a report that
32	  wrongly believes it warned). UNDER-crediting is a SAFE disclosed gap under always-release
33	  (I-perm-001). Every branch errs toward refusing credit.
34	
35	The binder forces the I-perm-002 SEMANTIC recognition ON internally (it is the whole point of the
36	binder) WITHOUT mutating the ``PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION`` global — it calls the shared
37	matcher with ``semantic=True``. The binder's own activation is the SEPARATE, default-OFF
38	``PG_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER`` flag, read at CALL TIME by the caller seam in ``native_gate_b_inputs`` (so
39	flag-OFF means no import and no call — byte-identical legacy behavior).
40	"""
41	
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import os
45	from typing import Any, Mapping, Sequence
46	
47	# The recognizer + canonical-match primitives live in native_gate_b_inputs (I-perm-002). Import them
48	# here at MODULE scope: this module is only ever imported by the caller seam AFTER the
49	# PG_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER flag is found truthy at call time, so flag-OFF never imports this module and
50	# never pulls these symbols (no import-time cost, no cycle — native_gate_b_inputs imports THIS module
51	# only via a function-local lazy import inside the seam).
52	from src.polaris_graph.roles.native_gate_b_inputs import (
53	    _KEY_ENTITY_ID,
54	    _content_requirements_satisfied_impl,
55	    _entity_canonical_match,
56	)
57	
58	# Validated-entity tuple shape produced by native_gate_b_inputs.build_native_gate_b_inputs:
59	# (entity_dict, severity, s0_category_or_None). Only S0 entities (s0_category is not None) are
60	# credit-eligible here; non-S0 coverage is handled by the existing _claim_covers_entity path and is
61	# intentionally NOT touched by this binder.
62	_SEVERITY_S0 = "S0"
63	
64	# Default-OFF activation flag. ON only on an EXPLICIT truthy token (a stray value like "garbage" must
65	# NOT silently enable it) — mirrors the _ON_VALUES discipline in release_policy.always_release_enabled.
66	_ENV_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER = "PG_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER"
67	_ON_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on"})
68	
69	
70	def s0_coverage_binder_enabled() -> bool:
71	    """``PG_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER`` (default OFF). Read at CALL TIME (never cached at import).
72	
73	    ON only on an explicit truthy token ('1'/'true'/'yes'/'on'); any other value (unset, '0',
74	    'false', 'no', 'off', or a stray 'garbage') keeps the binder OFF and the legacy path byte-identical.
75	    """
76	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER, "").strip().lower() in _ON_VALUES
77	
78	
79	def _claim_satisfies_s0_entity(
80	    claim_text: str,
81	    claim_evidence_records: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
82	    entity: Mapping[str, Any],
83	) -> bool:
84	    """The full S0 credit conjunction for ONE already-verified claim against ONE S0 entity.
85	
86	    Both halves must hold (fail-closed otherwise):
87	      (a) CANONICAL evidence match — the claim cites evidence whose DOI/PMID/full-URL EXACTLY matches
88	          the entity's declared canonical identifier (``_entity_canonical_match``). Citing the entity's
89	          source is the binding that ties the warning to the right substance/population pair.
90	      (b) CONTENT requirements satisfied with SEMANTIC recognition forced ON — every non-concept token
91	          (population anchor) literal-exact AND the contraindication concept token satisfied by a
92	          negation-guarded contraindication-DIRECTION phrase. ``semantic=True`` is passed explicitly so
93	          the binder does NOT depend on the global PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION flag, and does NOT
94	          mutate it.
95	    """
96	    if not any(_entity_canonical_match(dict(entity), rec) for rec in claim_evidence_records):
97	        return False
98	    return _content_requirements_satisfied_impl(claim_text, dict(entity), semantic=True)
99	
100	
101	def bind_s0_coverage(
102	    *,
103	    claim_text: str,
104	    claim_evidence_records: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
105	    validated_entities: Sequence[tuple[Mapping[str, Any], str, str | None]],
106	) -> tuple[set[str], set[str]]:
107	    """Credit S0 safety categories to ONE ALREADY-VERIFIED claim. Pure, additive, fail-closed.
108	
109	    The CALLER must invoke this ONLY for a strict_verify-VERIFIED claim (the ``verdict == VERIFIED``
110	    predicate in the credit rule is the caller's responsibility — the build seam only reaches this
111	    function for ``verification.is_verified`` sentences). This function then applies the remaining
112	    conjunction (canonical match + population anchor + contraindication-direction + negation guard)
113	    per S0 entity.
114	
115	    Returns ``(credited_categories, credited_element_ids)``:
116	      * ``credited_categories`` — the set of S0 ``s0_category`` strings this claim earns. The caller
117	        UNIONs these into the claim's ``s0_categories`` (idempotent set-union: harmless if a category
118	        is already present from the existing literal path or from PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION).
119	      * ``credited_element_ids`` — the S0 entity ids whose coverage this claim earns, so the caller can
120	        UNION them into ``covered_element_ids`` (keeping the D8 coverage fraction and the S0 must-cover
121	        gate in agreement — an S0 category credited as covered should also count its element as covered).
122	
123	    Crediting NOTHING (two empty sets) is the safe default for any claim that does not satisfy the full
124	    conjunction — never raised, never a silent over-credit.
125	    """
126	    credited_categories: set[str] = set()
127	    credited_element_ids: set[str] = set()
128	    for entity, severity, s0_category in validated_entities:
129	        if severity != _SEVERITY_S0 or s0_category is None:
130	            continue
131	        if _claim_satisfies_s0_entity(claim_text, claim_evidence_records, entity):
132	            credited_categories.add(s0_category)
133	            credited_element_ids.add(entity[_KEY_ENTITY_ID])
134	    return credited_categories, credited_element_ids
135	
136	
137	# ── WS-4 (beat-both Wave B): basket-membership entity-coverage FALLBACK ───────────────────────
138	# General ENTITY coverage (the completeness / coverage_fraction), NOT the S0 SAFETY floor. Credits a
139	# required entity when an ALREADY-VERIFIED claim cites an evidence_id that is a SUPPORTS member of the
140	# entity's OWN basket — i.e. the claim supports the entity through a corroborating source whose own
141	# canonical identifier differs from the entity's declared one (a §-1.3 basket carries the SAME claim
142	# across multiple sources). This is the second WS-4 leg (the first is the DOI-canonical tolerance in
143	# native_gate_b_inputs); it rides the SAME default-ON `PG_ENTITY_COVERAGE_CITATION_CREDIT` kill-switch
144	# (read by the caller seam, so flag-OFF never imports/calls this path).
145	#
146	# SAFETY (faithfulness-adjacent, highest care):
147	#   * SUPPORTS-ONLY, enforced HERE (never trusted): a REFUTES / NEUTRAL basket member NEVER credits
148	#     coverage — a source that refutes an entity must not mark it covered.
149	#   * VERIFIED-ONLY: the caller invokes this ONLY for a strict_verify-VERIFIED sentence, and the D8
150	#     coverage numerator downstream credits `covered_element_ids` only on a VERIFIED 4-role final
151	#     verdict — so a non-verified claim can never credit (additive credit, D8 still gates).
152	#   * NEVER an S0 SAFETY credit: this returns element_ids for the completeness ledger only; it never
153	#     credits an s0_category (the frozen S0 conjunction owns the safety floor). Under-crediting is a
154	#     SAFE disclosed gap; over-crediting safety would be lethal — this path can only under-, not over-,
155	#     credit the safety floor because it never touches it.
156	#   * FAIL-CLOSED: an entity with no SUPPORTS basket, or a claim citing none of its members, credits
157	#     nothing. A malformed basket member is skipped, never credited.
158	#
159	# The entity's basket is read from the entity dict — DEFAULT-ABSENT -> no credit (additive, never
160	# subtractive). Two accepted, forward-compatible shapes (upstream consolidation populates ONE):
161	#   * entity["supports_evidence_ids"]: a list of evidence_id strings ALREADY filtered to SUPPORTS.
162	#   * entity["evidence_basket"]: a list of {"evidence_id": str, "stance": str} members; ONLY the
163	#     stance == "SUPPORTS" members are eligible.
164	_KEY_SUPPORTS_EVIDENCE_IDS = "supports_evidence_ids"
165	_KEY_EVIDENCE_BASKET = "evidence_basket"
166	_KEY_MEMBER_EVIDENCE_ID = "evidence_id"
167	_KEY_MEMBER_STANCE = "stance"
168	_STANCE_SUPPORTS = "SUPPORTS"
169	
170	
171	def _entity_supports_basket(entity: Mapping[str, Any]) -> set[str]:
172	    """The set of evidence_ids that are SUPPORTS members of this entity's own basket.
173	
174	    Reads the two accepted shapes (see module note); the SUPPORTS-only rule is enforced HERE — a
175	    REFUTES / NEUTRAL / unknown-stance member is dropped. A missing/empty/malformed basket yields the
176	    empty set (fail-closed, no credit)."""
177	    ids: set[str] = set()
178	    raw_ids = entity.get(_KEY_SUPPORTS_EVIDENCE_IDS)
179	    if isinstance(raw_ids, (list, tuple, set)):
180	        for ev_id in raw_ids:
181	            if isinstance(ev_id, str) and ev_id.strip():
182	                ids.add(ev_id.strip())
183	    basket = entity.get(_KEY_EVIDENCE_BASKET)
184	    if isinstance(basket, (list, tuple)):
185	        for member in basket:
186	            if not isinstance(member, Mapping):
187	                continue
188	            stance = str(member.get(_KEY_MEMBER_STANCE, "")).strip().upper()
189	            if stance != _STANCE_SUPPORTS:
190	                continue
191	            ev_id = member.get(_KEY_MEMBER_EVIDENCE_ID)
192	            if isinstance(ev_id, str) and ev_id.strip():
193	                ids.add(ev_id.strip())
194	    return ids
195	
196	
197	def bind_basket_coverage(
198	    *,
199	    claim_evidence_ids: Sequence[str],
200	    validated_entities: Sequence[tuple[Mapping[str, Any], str, str | None]],
201	) -> set[str]:
202	    """Credit GENERAL entity coverage element_ids to ONE ALREADY-VERIFIED claim whose cited evidence
203	    is a SUPPORTS member of an entity's own basket. Pure, additive, fail-closed.
204	
205	    The CALLER must invoke this ONLY for a strict_verify-VERIFIED claim (the build seam reaches it only
206	    for `verification.is_verified` sentences). Returns the SET of covered element_ids (any severity) —
207	    for the completeness / coverage fraction only; it NEVER credits an S0 safety category. An entity
208	    with no SUPPORTS basket, or a claim citing none of its members, credits nothing (empty set)."""
209	    cited = {
210	        ev_id.strip()
211	        for ev_id in (claim_evidence_ids or [])
212	        if isinstance(ev_id, str) and ev_id.strip()
213	    }
214	    if not cited:
215	        return set()
216	    credited: set[str] = set()
217	    for entity, _severity, _s0_category in validated_entities:
218	        basket = _entity_supports_basket(entity)
219	        if basket and (cited & basket):
220	            credited.add(entity[_KEY_ENTITY_ID])
221	    return credited
222

1	"""Judge (Qwen3.6) verdict contract — hard-enum, NO silent default.
2	
3	Reuses the canonical 5-enum verdict shape from `claim_audit_scorer.Verdict`
4	(VERIFIED / PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE). It is IMPORTED, never
5	redefined, so there is exactly one verdict vocabulary in the codebase.
6	
7	The Judge runs self-hosted under vLLM with a hard enum constraint
8	(`structured_outputs.choice`, the correct spelling — `guided_choice` is deprecated, F4).
9	`JUDGE_CHOICES` exposes the exact allowed token list for that constraint (PR4).
10	
11	This module also holds the `source_missing` split classifier (`classify_unreachable`),
12	which separates a genuine artifact/fetch miss (UNREACHABLE) from a fabricated citation
13	identity (FABRICATED). The wiring into the release gate is sub-PR-3.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	from typing import Iterable, get_args
19	
20	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer import Verdict
21	
22	# The exact allowed verdict tokens, derived from the canonical Literal so the two can
23	# never drift. Used by PR4 for the vLLM `structured_outputs.choice` constraint.
24	JUDGE_CHOICES: list[str] = list(get_args(Verdict))
25	
26	# Fetch-miss subtypes: a real artifact/fetch failure for an IN-POOL source -> UNREACHABLE.
27	_FETCH_MISS_SUBTYPES = ("paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure")
28	
29	# Classification outputs of classify_unreachable.
30	_CLASS_UNREACHABLE = "UNREACHABLE"
31	_CLASS_FABRICATED = "FABRICATED"
32	
33	
34	class JudgeEnumError(ValueError):
35	    """Raised when a raw Judge output is not exactly one of the 5 canonical verdicts.
36	
37	    There is NO silent default. A non-enum token is a hard failure (clinical safety):
38	    the caller must treat it as a parse error and escalate, never coerce it to a verdict.
39	    """
40	
41	
42	def parse_judge_verdict(raw: str) -> Verdict:
43	    """Parse a raw Judge output into the canonical 5-enum `Verdict`.
44	
45	    The match is exact against `JUDGE_CHOICES` after stripping surrounding whitespace.
46	    Any non-enum token — garbage, empty, lowercase-unknown, partial — raises
47	    `JudgeEnumError`. There is deliberately NO case-folding and NO fuzzy match: the Judge
48	    is enum-constrained at decode time (vLLM), so anything off-enum signals a real fault.
49	    """
50	    if not isinstance(raw, str):
51	        raise JudgeEnumError(f"Judge output is not a string: {type(raw)!r}")
52	    token = raw.strip()
53	    if token not in JUDGE_CHOICES:
54	        raise JudgeEnumError(
55	            f"Judge output {token!r} is not one of the {len(JUDGE_CHOICES)} canonical "
56	            f"verdicts {JUDGE_CHOICES}"
57	        )
58	    # token is now a member of the Verdict Literal domain.
59	    return token  # type: ignore[return-value]
60	
61	
62	def classify_unreachable(
63	    subtype: str | None,
64	    citation_id: str | None,
65	    evidence_pool_ids: Iterable[str],
66	) -> str:
67	    """Split a 'source could not be confirmed' case into UNREACHABLE vs FABRICATED.
68	
69	    LOCKED PRECEDENCE (I-meta-002 iter-2 P1-b) — fabricated identity wins FIRST:
70	      (1) if `citation_id` is None/empty OR `citation_id` is NOT in `evidence_pool_ids`
71	          -> FABRICATED, regardless of `subtype`. A fabricated/unknown citation identity
72	          must NOT be laundered as a fetch failure.
73	      (2) ELSE if `subtype` in {paywall, robots, fetch_failure} -> UNREACHABLE.
74	
75	    `evidence_pool_ids` contract: it is the set of CANONICAL PRE-FETCH evidence IDs — every
76	    source the pipeline selected/attempted, INCLUDING records whose fetch later FAILED
77	    (paywall/robots/fetch_failure). A genuine fetch failure therefore still has its
78	    `citation_id` present in the pool -> correctly UNREACHABLE; only an id that was never a
79	    selected source -> FABRICATED.
80	
81	    Anything else (an in-pool id whose subtype is not a known fetch-miss, e.g.
82	    `source_missing`/None/unknown) is OUT of this contract's domain and is a programming
83	    error, so it raises rather than silently defaulting (LAW II fail-loud).
84	    """
85	    pool = set(evidence_pool_ids)
86	    # (1) fabricated identity check — evaluated BEFORE the subtype branch.
87	    if not citation_id or citation_id not in pool:
88	        return _CLASS_FABRICATED
89	    # (2) in-pool id: a real fetch miss is UNREACHABLE.
90	    if subtype in _FETCH_MISS_SUBTYPES:
91	        return _CLASS_UNREACHABLE
92	    raise ValueError(
93	        f"classify_unreachable: in-pool citation {citation_id!r} with subtype {subtype!r} "
94	        f"is outside the fetch-miss contract domain {_FETCH_MISS_SUBTYPES}"
95	    )
96

1	"""Judge (Qwen3.6) adapter — terminal-arbiter request builder + LOUD-FAIL caller.
2	
3	The Judge is the terminal arbiter: it sees the claim, the evidence, and the Mirror +
4	Sentinel signals, and emits exactly one of the 5 canonical verdicts. It runs self-hosted
5	under vLLM with a HARD ENUM constraint at decode time — `structured_outputs.choice =
6	JUDGE_CHOICES` (current vLLM spelling; `guided_choice` is DEPRECATED, F4). Qwen context is
7	BOUNDED (`max_tokens`); do not assume unbounded.
8	
9	FAIL LOUD (NOT closed): `parse_judge_verdict` raises `JudgeEnumError` on any non-enum token.
10	`run_judge` deliberately does NOT wrap that — a missing/garbage arbiter verdict must
11	propagate, never coerce to a default. (Sentinel/Mirror fail CLOSED; the Judge fails LOUD —
12	do not pattern-copy a fail-closed wrap here.)
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import hashlib
18	import json
19	import logging
20	import os
21	import threading
22	from typing import Any
23	
24	from src.polaris_graph.roles.judge_contract import (
25	    JUDGE_CHOICES,
26	    JudgeEnumError,
27	    Verdict,
28	    parse_judge_verdict,
29	)
30	from src.polaris_graph.roles.openai_compatible_transport import RoleTransportError
31	from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import (
32	    RoleCallRecord,
33	    RoleRequest,
34	    RoleResponse,
35	    RoleTransport,
36	)
37	
38	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
39	
40	_ROLE = "judge"
41	
42	# I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C.2: the fail-closed verdict a force-closed Judge transport maps to.
43	# UNSUPPORTED is the SAME fail-closed verdict the Mirror-fail and Sentinel-unsafe paths already
44	# converge on (`role_pipeline._compose_final_verdict`) — a non-credited (uncovered) claim, never a
45	# synthesized PASS, never None. `_compose_final_verdict` returns `raw_judge_verdict` on the
46	# sentinel-grounded path, so the verdict MUST be a concrete token (a None there yields no verdict).
47	_JUDGE_FAIL_CLOSED_VERDICT: Verdict = "UNSUPPORTED"
48	
49	# I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C: the single seam degrade switch (default ON). Generalizes the
50	# Sentinel precedent; `PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE` is honored as a back-compat ALIAS so an
51	# operator who only set the old switch still degrades the Judge identically. Read at CALL time.
52	_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ENV = "PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE"
53	_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ALIAS_ENV = "PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE"
54	_DEGRADE_OFF_TOKENS = ("0", "false", "no", "off")
55	
56	
57	def _role_transport_degrade_enabled() -> bool:
58	    """Whether a force-closed Judge `RoleTransportError` degrades to per-claim UNSUPPORTED (default ON).
59	
60	    OFF iff `PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE` is explicitly off; the legacy `PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE`
61	    is honored as a back-compat ALIAS (off only if it is explicitly off AND the new switch is unset),
62	    so an operator who set only the old switch to off still hard-halts the Judge arm consistently.
63	    LAW VI: env-driven, read lazily so a slate override after import wins."""
64	    primary = os.getenv(_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ENV)
65	    if primary is not None:
66	        return primary.strip().lower() not in _DEGRADE_OFF_TOKENS
67	    alias = os.getenv(_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ALIAS_ENV)
68	    if alias is not None:
69	        return alias.strip().lower() not in _DEGRADE_OFF_TOKENS
70	    return True
71	
72	# Bounded Qwen context (F4: do not assume unbounded). The verdict is a single enum token,
73	# so a small ceiling is sufficient and keeps the arbiter from drifting into prose.
74	_DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 16
75	
76	# vLLM hard-enum spec key (current spelling, NOT guided_choice).
77	_STRUCTURED_OUTPUTS_KEY = "structured_outputs"
78	_CHOICE_KEY = "choice"
79	
80	_ARBITER_INSTRUCTION = (
81	    "You are the terminal arbiter. Given the claim, the evidence, and the Mirror and "
82	    "Sentinel signals below, output exactly one verdict token and nothing else."
83	)
