303	def validate_entity_severity(
304	    entity: dict, d8_config: D8PolicyConfig
305	) -> tuple[str, str | None]:
306	    """§7.E schema-validator (FAIL CLOSED — Codex P1 iter1). Never defaults to S3.
307	
308	    Returns (severity, s0_category_or_None). Raises on a missing/invalid severity, an entity
309	    that declares NO builder-recognized canonical identifier (doi/pmid/url_pattern — else it
310	    would be silently uncoverable), an S0 entity without a valid `s0_category` in the D8
311	    must-cover vocabulary, or an S0 entity without a non-empty `coverage_content_requirements`.
312	    """
313	    severity = entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_SEVERITY)
314	    if severity not in _VALID_SEVERITIES:
315	        raise ValueError(
316	            f"validate_entity_severity: entity {entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_ID)!r} has "
317	            f"severity={severity!r}; must be one of {_VALID_SEVERITIES} (NEVER default S3)."
318	        )
319	    # Every required entity MUST declare at least one canonical identifier the builder can
320	    # match on (doi / pmid / url_pattern). An identifier-less entity is permanently
321	    # uncoverable — make that a LOUD config error rather than a silent coverage loss.
322	    if not any(entity.get(key) for key in _ENTITY_CANONICAL_KEYS):
323	        raise ValueError(
324	            f"validate_entity_severity: entity {entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_ID)!r} declares no "
325	            f"canonical identifier among {_ENTITY_CANONICAL_KEYS}; it would be permanently "
326	            f"uncoverable (fail-closed)."
327	        )
328	    if severity != _SEVERITY_S0:
329	        return severity, None
330	
331	    s0_category = entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_S0_CATEGORY)
332	    if not s0_category or s0_category not in d8_config.s0_must_cover_categories:
333	        raise ValueError(
334	            f"validate_entity_severity: S0 entity {entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_ID)!r} declares "
335	            f"s0_category={s0_category!r} which is missing or not in the D8 must-cover "
336	            f"vocabulary {tuple(d8_config.s0_must_cover_categories)} (fail-closed)."
337	        )
338	    content_reqs = entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_CONTENT_REQS)
339	    if not isinstance(content_reqs, list) or not content_reqs:
340	        raise ValueError(
341	            f"validate_entity_severity: S0 entity {entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_ID)!r} has no "
342	            f"non-empty {_KEY_ENTITY_CONTENT_REQS}; an S0 safety category requires "
343	            f"deterministic content tokens to credit coverage (fail-closed)."
344	        )
345	    # Every requirement token must be a NON-BLANK string. A list like [''] or ['   '] or
346	    # [123] would otherwise pass the truthy-list check above and then let a bare canonical
347	    # citation wrongly earn S0 credit (Codex P1: never validate an S0 entity whose content
348	    # requirements are empty/blank/non-string after .strip()).
349	    for token in content_reqs:
350	        if not isinstance(token, str) or not token.strip():
351	            raise ValueError(
352	                f"validate_entity_severity: S0 entity {entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_ID)!r} has a "
353	                f"{_KEY_ENTITY_CONTENT_REQS} element {token!r} that is not a non-blank string; "
354	                f"every S0 content requirement must be a real deterministic token (fail-closed)."
355	            )
356	    return severity, s0_category
357	
358	
359	# --- WS-4 (beat-both Wave B): DOI-tolerant entity coverage credit --------------------------------
360	# ROOT CAUSE (drb_72): a required entity that is DOI-only (bare `doi`, empty `url_pattern`) went
361	# UN-credited on genuinely-VERIFIED claims, so `required_entity_ledger` reported coverage_fraction
362	# 4/7=0.571 and listed VERIFIED entities as gaps. TWO independent gaps caused it, BOTH in this file:
363	#   (1) `normalize_evidence_pool_lookup` only regex-extracted a DOI from `source_url`/text and NEVER
364	#       read the raw record's STRUCTURED `doi`/`pmid` field. drb_72 entities [2]/[3]/[4] carry the DOI
365	#       ONLY in that structured field (their `source_url` is None or DOI-less), so the normalized
366	#       record reached `_entity_canonical_match` with NO doi at all -> no match, un-covered.
367	#   (2) `_entity_canonical_match` compared identifiers by RAW EXACT equality, so a DOI expressed as a
368	#       `https://doi.org/…` resolver URL (or in a different case) never equalled the bare-token DOI on
369	#       the other side even when they name the SAME work.
370	# Both fixes ride ONE default-ON kill-switch `PG_ENTITY_COVERAGE_CITATION_CREDIT`; OFF => the exact
371	# pre-WS-4 behavior, byte-identical (0.571 on the fixture). ADDITIVE ONLY: a DOI is a precise WORK
372	# identifier — canonical-DOI equality is NEVER a substring/fragment match (two sources sharing a DOI
373	# ARE the same work), so this cannot over-credit; and the D8 4-role VERIFIED filter downstream
374	# (sweep_integration credits `covered_element_ids` only on a VERIFIED final verdict) is untouched, so
375	# a NON-verified claim can never credit coverage.
376	_ENV_ENTITY_COVERAGE_CITATION_CREDIT = "PG_ENTITY_COVERAGE_CITATION_CREDIT"
377	# Default-ON off-token idiom (mirrors release_policy.always_release_enabled): OFF only on an EXPLICIT
378	# off token; unset / empty / unrecognized resolves to ON so the fix cannot be silently disabled by a
379	# stray value. Read at CALL TIME (never cached) so an OFF run is byte-identical to legacy.
380	_COVERAGE_CREDIT_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off"})
381	# DOI resolver prefixes stripped to reach the bare canonical DOI token. A DOI is officially
382	# case-insensitive and a doi.org / dx.doi.org URL is just a resolver wrapper around the same token.
383	_DOI_RESOLVER_PREFIXES = (
384	    "https://doi.org/",
385	    "http://doi.org/",
386	    "https://dx.doi.org/",
387	    "http://dx.doi.org/",
388	)
389	# Structured identifier keys on the RAW evidence-pool row (read only under the flag).
390	_RAW_STRUCTURED_DOI_KEY = "doi"
391	_RAW_STRUCTURED_PMID_KEY = "pmid"
392	
393	
394	def _entity_coverage_citation_credit_enabled() -> bool:
395	    """``PG_ENTITY_COVERAGE_CITATION_CREDIT`` — default ON (WS-4). OFF only on an EXPLICIT off token
396	    ('0'/'false'/'no'/'off'); unset / empty / unrecognized -> ON. OFF reproduces the pre-WS-4
397	    exact-match behavior byte-identically (0.571 on the drb_72 fixture).
398	    """
399	    return (
400	        os.environ.get(_ENV_ENTITY_COVERAGE_CITATION_CREDIT, "").strip().lower()

400	        os.environ.get(_ENV_ENTITY_COVERAGE_CITATION_CREDIT, "").strip().lower()
401	        not in _COVERAGE_CREDIT_OFF_VALUES
402	    )
403	
404	
405	def _canonical_doi(value: Any) -> str:
406	    """Return the bare, lowercased canonical DOI for a value, or '' if it is not a DOI.
407	
408	    Strips a doi.org / dx.doi.org resolver prefix (case-insensitive) then lowercases (DOIs are
409	    officially case-insensitive). Returns '' unless the remainder starts with '10.' — so a plain
410	    non-DOI URL / fragment / domain can NEVER masquerade as a DOI match (fail-closed: a fragment can
411	    never equal a full bare DOI). Precise-identifier equality, never substring — so it cannot
412	    over-credit.
413	    """
414	    if value in (None, ""):
415	        return ""
416	    text = str(value).strip()
417	    lowered = text.lower()
418	    for prefix in _DOI_RESOLVER_PREFIXES:
419	        if lowered.startswith(prefix):
420	            text = text[len(prefix):].strip()
421	            break
422	    text = text.lower()
423	    if not text.startswith("10."):
424	        return ""
425	    return text

428	def _entity_canonical_match(entity: dict, record: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
429	    """EXACT canonical-identifier match (P2 #4): DOI / PMID / full canonical URL.
430	
431	    Equality per (entity_key, record_key) pair only — never substring. The URL pair compares
432	    the entity's `url_pattern` against the record's resolved `url`; a broad `url_pattern`
433	    FRAGMENT can never equal a full resolved URL, so it fails closed.
434	
435	    WS-4 (PG_ENTITY_COVERAGE_CITATION_CREDIT, default ON): after the exact pairs, add a DOI-CANONICAL
436	    tolerance leg. A DOI carried as a `https://doi.org/…` / `http://dx.doi.org/…` resolver URL — in
437	    the entity's `doi` OR `url_pattern`, or the record's `doi` OR `url` — or in a different case, is
438	    canonicalized (`_canonical_doi`: strip resolver prefix + lowercase) and matched on the bare DOI
439	    token. This is precise WORK-identity equality (two sources sharing a DOI ARE the same work), NOT a
440	    substring/fragment match, so it cannot over-credit. Flag OFF -> this leg is skipped, byte-identical
441	    to the exact-equality behavior.
442	    """
443	    for entity_key, record_key in _CANONICAL_MATCH_PAIRS:
444	        entity_value = entity.get(entity_key)
445	        if entity_value in (None, ""):
446	            continue
447	        record_value = record.get(record_key)
448	        if record_value in (None, ""):
449	            continue
450	        if str(entity_value).strip() == str(record_value).strip():
451	            return True
452	    if _entity_coverage_citation_credit_enabled():
453	        entity_doi = _canonical_doi(entity.get("doi")) or _canonical_doi(entity.get("url_pattern"))
454	        if entity_doi:
455	            record_doi = _canonical_doi(record.get("doi")) or _canonical_doi(record.get("url"))
456	            if record_doi and entity_doi == record_doi:
457	                return True
458	    return False

461	# --- I-perm-002 (#1196): semantic contraindication credit (default OFF -> literal-exact) -----
462	# The CDC contraindication source for `probiotic_immunocompromised_contraindication` says probiotics
463	# "should be avoided for patients who ... are immunocompromised" — it NEVER uses the word
464	# "contraindicated". So a FAITHFUL claim grounded in that span (e.g. drb_76 claim 03-001, "not
465	# recommended for patients who are immunocompromised", VERIFIED + cites the exact CDC url) can never
466	# contain the literal token, and the S0 `contraindications` category goes un-credited -> the safety
467	# floor reports insufficient even though the report DID warn. PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION
468	# relaxes ONLY the contraindication-CONCEPT requirement token to a curated, high-precision set of
469	# contraindication-DIRECTION phrases; the population token (e.g. "immunocompromised") is NEVER
470	# relaxed — it stays the literal precision anchor that binds substance+population (R6). A
471	# deterministic negation guard refuses credit on any opposite-direction / negated phrase so a
472	# faithful-but-INVERTED claim can never earn S0 safety credit.
473	#
474	# Direction of error is deliberate: over-crediting a contraindication is §-1.1-LETHAL (a report that
475	# wrongly believes it warned); UNDER-crediting is a SAFE disclosed gap under always-release
476	# (I-perm-001). Every layer therefore errs toward refusing credit. Four independent guards stand
477	# between a claim and S0 credit: (1) `verification.is_verified` — only strict-verified claims reach
478	# the matcher; (2) `_entity_canonical_match` — the claim must cite the entity's EXACT source;
479	# (3) the literal population anchor; (4) this negation guard.
480	_ENV_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION = "PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION"
481	_SEMANTIC_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "no", "off"})
482	# Requirement tokens that NAME the contraindication concept — only these are relaxed to synonyms.
483	# Both singular AND plural ("contraindications") so a future config token is also relaxed (Codex P2).
484	_CONTRAINDICATION_CONCEPT_TOKENS = frozenset(
485	    {"contraindicated", "contraindication", "contraindications"}
486	)
487	# "not recommended" warns UNLESS the "recommend AGAINST" idiom inverts it ("not recommended against
488	# use" == recommended FOR use). The inverting "against" can be interposed at ANY distance ("not
489	# recommended by the CDC source against use"), so a FIXED tail window is gameable (Codex P0-3): a
490	# "not recommended" occurrence counts ONLY when NO "against" follows it anywhere in the remaining
491	# claim text (whole-tail scan). Over-refusing a warning that merely mentions "against" downstream is
492	# a SAFE under-credit. Handled separately from the imperatives below, which carry no such idiom.
493	_RECOMMEND_DIRECTIONAL_INDICATOR = "not recommended"
494	_RECOMMEND_INVERTER = "against"
495	# DIRECTIONAL IMPERATIVES — each asserts an action-prohibition for the population ("do not give this
496	# to this group"). Robustly positive: each already encodes a negation/avoidance, so inverting one in
497	# place is unnatural — presence => contraindication direction. Bare "avoid"/"unsafe"/"risk" are
498	# deliberately EXCLUDED (too easily negated / ambiguous). The bare concept stem is handled separately
499	# below (it is a noun and far easier to invert).
500	_CONTRAINDICATION_DIRECTIONAL_INDICATORS = (
501	    "should be avoided",
502	    "should not be used",
503	    "should not be given",
504	    "should not be administered",
505	    "must not be used",
506	    "must not be given",
507	    "must not be administered",
508	    "not be used in",
509	)
510	# The bare contraindication CONCEPT stem (matches "contraindicated" / "contraindication(s)"). A noun
511	# is easily negated — BEFORE it ("no known contraindications", "not generally / clearly
512	# contraindicated", "need not be contraindicated", "without contraindication") or AFTER it
513	# ("contraindications are unknown / not established / absent"). The brittle contiguous-phrase list
514	# this replaces MISSED every interposed-qualifier form (Codex P0-1: it credited "CDC reports no known
515	# contraindications ... in immunocompromised patients"). The stem counts as a positive direction
516	# ONLY when NO negation context surrounds ANY occurrence — one negated mention disqualifies the stem
517	# signal for the whole claim (conservative = SAFE under-credit, never lethal over-credit).
518	_CONTRAINDICATION_STEM = "contraindicat"
519	# Interposition between the negator and the stem (qualifier adverbs / short clauses). Tight 20-char
520	# window so a SHORT inverting qualifier ("no known", "not generally") is caught while an unrelated
521	# distant negation ("no doubt probiotics are contraindicated") is NOT — that long form stays a credit.
522	_CONTRA_NEG_INTERPOSE = r"[\w\s,'()/-]{0,20}?"
523	_CONTRAINDICATION_NEGATION_RE = re.compile(
524	    # negator BEFORE the stem (contractions are expanded to `X not` first, so `\bnot\b` covers
525	    # aren't/isn't/haven't/etc.; "free of"/"devoid of"/"zero" are short absence forms)
526	    r"\b(?:no|not|never|without|none|neither|nor|lack|lacks|lacking|few|rare|rarely|absent|zero|"
527	    r"free\s+of|devoid\s+of|unknown|unestablished|unproven|undetermined)\b"
528	    + _CONTRA_NEG_INTERPOSE + _CONTRAINDICATION_STEM
529	    # OR a negation/absence predicate AFTER the stem
530	    + r"|" + _CONTRAINDICATION_STEM + r"\w*" + _CONTRA_NEG_INTERPOSE
531	    + r"\b(?:unknown|undetermined|unestablished|not\s+(?:been\s+)?established|absent|unclear|"
532	    r"unlikely|none|negligible|rare|few)\b",
533	    re.IGNORECASE,
534	)
535	# Negative contractions -> expanded `... not` (a SPACE before "not" so the `\bnot\b` negator fires;
536	# "cannot" would join the word boundary). Apostrophe is normalized to ASCII first so a curly
537	# right-single-quote (U+2019) in scraped prose does not defeat the lookup. Expanding ALSO helps the
538	# directional route ("shouldn't be used" -> "should not be used" matches the indicator). Codex P0-2:
539	# the pre/post regex only knew expanded forms, so `aren't contraindicated` / `haven't been
540	# established` over-credited.
541	_NEGATIVE_CONTRACTIONS = {
542	    "aren't": "are not", "isn't": "is not", "wasn't": "was not", "weren't": "were not",
543	    "haven't": "have not", "hasn't": "has not", "hadn't": "had not",
544	    "don't": "do not", "doesn't": "does not", "didn't": "did not",
545	    "won't": "will not", "wouldn't": "would not", "can't": "can not", "couldn't": "could not",
546	    "shouldn't": "should not", "mustn't": "must not", "needn't": "need not",
547	    "mightn't": "might not", "shan't": "shall not", "oughtn't": "ought not",
548	}
549	
550	
551	def _expand_negative_contractions(text: str) -> str:
552	    """Normalize the curly apostrophe to ASCII, then expand negative contractions to `X not`."""
553	    text = text.replace("’", "'")
554	    for contraction, expanded in _NEGATIVE_CONTRACTIONS.items():
555	        text = text.replace(contraction, expanded)
556	    return text
557

560	    """``PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION`` (default OFF -> literal-exact content match)."""
561	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION, "").strip().lower() not in _SEMANTIC_OFF_VALUES

564	# I-perm-020 (#1212): the S0 coverage-binder activation flag. Read HERE at CALL TIME (never at import)
565	# so flag-OFF means the coverage_binder module is NEVER imported and the seam stays byte-identical. ON
566	# only on an explicit truthy token (a stray value must NOT silently enable it). The flag NAME mirrors
567	# `coverage_binder._ENV_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER`; this reader is duplicated (not imported) precisely so the
568	# OFF path never pulls the binder module.
569	_ENV_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER = "PG_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER"
570	_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER_ON_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on"})
571	
572	
573	def _s0_coverage_binder_enabled() -> bool:

575	    return (
576	        os.environ.get(_ENV_S0_COVERAGE_BINDER, "").strip().lower()
577	        in _S0_COVERAGE_BINDER_ON_VALUES
578	    )

581	def _contraindication_direction_present(lowered_claim: str) -> bool:
582	    """True iff the claim asserts a contraindication DIRECTION for the population (not its negation).
583	
584	    Three independent positive routes, all fail-closed against inversion (over-crediting a
585	    contraindication is §-1.1-lethal; under-crediting is a safe disclosed gap under always-release):
586	
587	    1. "not recommended" — UNLESS an "against" follows it anywhere downstream (the "recommend
588	       against" idiom inverts it; whole-tail scan, not a gameable fixed window — Codex P0-3).
589	    2. A DIRECTIONAL IMPERATIVE ("should be avoided", "should not be used", ...) — no "against"-style
590	       in-place inverter applies, so presence => direction.
591	    3. The bare concept STEM ("contraindicat...") — only when NO negation context (pre OR post)
592	       surrounds any occurrence; a single negated mention disqualifies the stem for the whole claim.
593	
594	    Negative contractions are expanded first (`aren't` -> `are not`) so the guard is not defeated by
595	    contraction spelling (Codex P0-2).
596	    """
597	    lowered_claim = _expand_negative_contractions(lowered_claim)
598	    start = lowered_claim.find(_RECOMMEND_DIRECTIONAL_INDICATOR)
599	    while start != -1:
600	        tail = lowered_claim[start + len(_RECOMMEND_DIRECTIONAL_INDICATOR):]
601	        if _RECOMMEND_INVERTER not in tail:
602	            return True
603	        start = lowered_claim.find(_RECOMMEND_DIRECTIONAL_INDICATOR, start + 1)
604	    if any(indicator in lowered_claim for indicator in _CONTRAINDICATION_DIRECTIONAL_INDICATORS):
605	        return True
606	    if _CONTRAINDICATION_STEM in lowered_claim and not _CONTRAINDICATION_NEGATION_RE.search(
607	        lowered_claim
608	    ):
609	        return True
610	    return False

613	def _content_requirements_satisfied_impl(
614	    claim_text: str, entity: dict, *, semantic: bool
615	) -> bool:
616	    """Shared content-requirement matcher with an EXPLICIT semantic flag (no env read).
617	
618	    All `coverage_content_requirements` present in the claim (case-insensitive, exact). FAIL CLOSED
619	    (Codex P1): if the requirements list is empty — or somehow all-blank — return False rather than
620	    vacuously True. A bare canonical-evidence citation must NEVER earn S0 credit without a real
621	    deterministic content match. (Validation in `validate_entity_severity` already blocks empty/blank
622	    S0 requirements at load; this is the matcher-side backstop.)
623	
624	    I-perm-002 (#1196): when `semantic` is True, a requirement token that NAMES the contraindication
625	    concept ("contraindicated"/"contraindication(s)") is satisfied by any high-precision
626	    contraindication-DIRECTION phrase (negation-guarded); every OTHER token (e.g. "immunocompromised")
627	    stays literal-exact. `semantic=False` -> byte-identical literal-exact match. The semantic decision
628	    is taken by the CALLER (the public `_content_requirements_satisfied` reads the
629	    PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION env flag; the I-perm-020 coverage_binder forces it True), so this
630	    impl never reads the environment — keeping the credit policy at the call sites, not buried here.
631	    """
632	    lowered = claim_text.lower()
633	    requirements = [
634	        token
635	        for token in entity.get(_KEY_ENTITY_CONTENT_REQS) or []
636	        if isinstance(token, str) and token.strip()
637	    ]
638	    if not requirements:
639	        return False
640	    for token in requirements:
641	        token_lower = token.lower()
642	        if semantic and token_lower in _CONTRAINDICATION_CONCEPT_TOKENS:
643	            if not _contraindication_direction_present(lowered):
644	                return False
645	        elif token_lower not in lowered:
646	            return False
647	    return True
648	
649	
650	def _content_requirements_satisfied(claim_text: str, entity: dict) -> bool:
651	    """All `coverage_content_requirements` present in the claim (case-insensitive, exact).
652	
653	    Thin wrapper over `_content_requirements_satisfied_impl` that takes the semantic decision from the
654	    `PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_CONTRAINDICATION` env flag (default OFF -> byte-identical literal-exact match).
655	    Behavior is unchanged from the pre-refactor function: OFF reads identical, ON relaxes ONLY the
656	    contraindication concept token to a negation-guarded direction phrase.
657	    """
658	    return _content_requirements_satisfied_impl(
659	        claim_text, entity, semantic=_semantic_contraindication_enabled()
660	    )

663	def _claim_covers_entity(
664	    claim_evidence_records: list[Mapping[str, Any]],
665	    claim_text: str,
666	    entity: dict,
667	    *,
668	    is_s0: bool,
669	) -> bool:
670	    """Coverage test (§7.D, Codex-tightened + P2 #4).
671	
672	    ENTITY coverage: True iff the claim cites evidence whose CANONICAL identifier EXACTLY
673	    matches the entity's. anchor-string-in-sentence alone does NOT grant coverage.
674	
675	    S0 SAFETY-CATEGORY coverage (stricter): an S0 entity is covered ONLY when BOTH (a) the
676	    canonical evidence match holds AND (b) the claim text deterministically satisfies the
677	    entity's `coverage_content_requirements`. A broad source citation without the content
678	    match does NOT satisfy an S0 safety category.
679	    """
680	    if not any(_entity_canonical_match(entity, rec) for rec in claim_evidence_records):
681	        return False
682	    if is_s0 and not _content_requirements_satisfied(claim_text, entity):
683	        return False
684	    return True

687	# FX-03 / I-ready-017 (#1107): the 4-role seam (Sentinel decomposition + Judge) joins each
688	# EvidenceDocument.text as the SPAN the claim is checked against. Pre-FX-03 the WHOLE record text
689	# was passed, so a claim could be graded VERIFIED on support living ANYWHERE in the document, not
690	# in the cited [start:end] window (BUG-02: confirmed out-of-span false-accept, claim 06-004).
691	_GATE_B_CITED_SPAN_ENV = "PG_GATE_B_CITED_SPAN"
692	_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_ENV = "PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES"
693	_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_DEFAULT = 400
694	
695	# I-perm-022 (#1214): normalize the cited SPAN text BEFORE the four-role second evaluators
696	# (Sentinel decomposition + Judge) read it, so a PDF-extraction LIGATURE does not flip a
697	# genuinely-supported atom to "unsupported" (a false negative surfaced as [confidence:low]).
698	#
699	# LIGATURE-ONLY is the ONLY §-1.1-safe span repair. A Latin presentation-form ligature is a
700	# SINGLE codepoint that decomposes to a FIXED letter sequence with NO word-boundary change, so
701	# it can neither join nor split words and therefore cannot fabricate support on a genuine
702	# negative — it only renders the codepoint as the letters an LLM evaluator should have read.
703	# De-hyphenation and zero-width handling were DELIBERATELY DROPPED as §-1.1-UNSAFE (Codex
704	# brief-gate iter-2 P1): any JOIN ("re-\nsigned" -> "resigned"; "not<ZWSP>able" -> "notable")
705	# or SPLIT ("in<ZWSP>effective" -> "in effective") changes meaning and can make an unsupported
706	# claim appear present. ZERO digit modification (ligature codepoints carry no digits).
707	_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE_ENV = "PG_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE"
708	# DERIVE the ligature->letters map from Unicode NFKD (the authoritative compatibility
709	# decomposition), NOT a hand-typed table — a hand value can be wrong (e.g. U+FB05 LONG-S-T
710	# decomposes to "st", not "ft"; mis-typing it would fabricate "loft" from "lost", Codex
711	# brief-gate iter-3 P1). Only codepoints whose NFKD is pure ASCII letters are kept, so a
712	# ligature is ALWAYS replaced by its exact letters with NO word-boundary change.
713	_LIGATURE_MAP = {
714	    chr(cp): unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", chr(cp))
715	    for cp in range(0xFB00, 0xFB07)  # FB00..FB06: ff fi fl ffi ffl st st
716	    if unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", chr(cp)).isascii()
717	    and unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", chr(cp)).isalpha()
718	}
719	_LIGATURE_RE = re.compile("[" + "".join(_LIGATURE_MAP) + "]")
720	
721	
722	def _span_normalize_enabled() -> bool:
723	    return os.getenv(_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE_ENV, "0").strip() == "1"
724	
725	
726	def _normalize_span_text(text: str) -> str:
727	    """Repair ONLY Latin presentation-form ligatures (U+FB00..U+FB06) in a cited span:
728	    each is a single codepoint -> its canonical letters, with NO word-boundary change and
729	    ZERO digit modification. Flag OFF (default) -> input returned unchanged (byte-identical).
730	    I-perm-022 (#1214)."""
731	    if not _span_normalize_enabled() or not text:
732	        return text
733	    return _LIGATURE_RE.sub(lambda m: _LIGATURE_MAP[m.group(0)], text)
734

736	# --- V4 (I-deepfix-001): whole-basket D8 adjudication input (§-1.3 BASKET FAITHFULNESS) ---------
737	# ROOT CAUSE (drb_72): the D8 four-role seam (Sentinel decomposition + Judge) adjudicates a claim
738	# against ONLY the bare cited-span text of each cited source, stripped of that source's PROVENANCE
739	# identity. A claim's ATTRIBUTION atom ("Frey and Osborne developed a novel methodology ...") is
740	# then marked `unsupported` by the Sentinel because the abstract SPAN self-references in the first
741	# person ("We examine ...") and never re-names its own authors -> the Sentinel returns UNGROUNDED
742	# -> the LOCKED `_compose_final_verdict` step-2 override downgrades a Judge VERIFIED to UNSUPPORTED
743	# (measured: claim 01-008 frey_osborne_computerisation). The source RECORD, however, literally
744	# carries `authors: ['Frey C', 'Osborne M']` — the attribution IS grounded in the cited work's OWN
745	# declared identity, part of its basket, that the bare span throws away.
746	#
747	# FIX (§-1.3 BASKET FAITHFULNESS — decide a claim against its WHOLE basket, never a single bare
748	# span): the adjudication input for EACH cited source in the claim's basket carries that source's
749	# DECLARED PROVENANCE IDENTITY (authors / year / venue / doi) as a bounded, clearly-labeled header
750	# PREPENDED to its FX-03 cited-span window. An attribution / identity atom then grounds against the
751	# source's real authorship, recovering the single-span false-negative. The verdict CARRIES the
752	# basket corroboration (source count + tier weights) in the audit_map.
753	#
754	# STRENGTHENS, ADMITS NOTHING UNVERIFIED (the binding safety argument, faithfulness-CRITICAL):
755	#   * The header carries ONLY bibliographic IDENTITY (WHO wrote it, WHEN, WHERE, its DOI) — NEVER
756	#     the source TITLE and NEVER any body/abstract prose. It can therefore ground a WHO/WHEN/WHERE
757	#     attribution atom, but it carries NO factual/effect assertion, so it can NEVER ground a WHAT
758	#     (numeric / mechanism / effect) atom. A claim whose FACTS are unsupported by the cited span
759	#     still fails — the body window is UNCHANGED (FX-03 / BUG-02 out-of-span defense preserved
760	#     byte-for-byte; only an identity header is prepended, the window bytes are untouched).
761	#   * The header shows the source's TRUE authors, so a MIS-attribution (a claim naming an author
762	#     the source does not have) still fails closed — the header can only ground a TRUE attribution.
763	#   * A source that declares NO provenance identity yields an EMPTY header -> the adjudication input
764	#     is byte-identical to the bare span (no fabricated grounding, no vacuous credit).
765	# Default-ON kill-switch `PG_GATE_B_WHOLE_BASKET`; OFF -> byte-identical to the pre-V4 bare-span seam.
766	_GATE_B_WHOLE_BASKET_ENV = "PG_GATE_B_WHOLE_BASKET"
767	_WHOLE_BASKET_OFF_TOKENS = ("0", "false", "no", "off")
768	# Structured metadata keys carried from the raw evidence row into the normalized record so the header
769	# builder can read them. IDENTITY ONLY (never `title`, never `direct_quote`/`statement` body). `tier`
770	# is carried for the corroboration WEIGHT surfaced in the audit_map, NOT for the header text.
771	_PROVENANCE_HEADER_LABEL = "cited source provenance"
772	
773	
774	def _whole_basket_enabled() -> bool:
775	    """``PG_GATE_B_WHOLE_BASKET`` — default ON (V4). OFF only on an EXPLICIT off token
776	    ('0'/'false'/'no'/'off'); unset / empty / unrecognized -> ON. Read at CALL TIME (LAW VI) so a
777	    slate/test override after import wins. OFF -> byte-identical to the pre-V4 bare-span seam."""
778	    return os.getenv(_GATE_B_WHOLE_BASKET_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in _WHOLE_BASKET_OFF_TOKENS
779

781	def _format_authors(authors: Any) -> str:
782	    """Render the record's `authors` field to a compact identity string, or '' if none.
783	
784	    Accepts a list/tuple of names (the evidence-pool shape, e.g. ['Frey C', 'Osborne M']) or a bare
785	    string; drops blanks. This is a WHO identity string, never a factual assertion."""
786	    if isinstance(authors, str):
787	        return authors.strip()
788	    if isinstance(authors, (list, tuple)):
789	        return ", ".join(str(a).strip() for a in authors if str(a).strip())
790	    return ""

793	def _provenance_header(record: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
794	    """Build the bounded, labeled source-IDENTITY header for the whole-basket adjudication input.
795	
796	    Carries WHO (authors) / WHEN (year) / WHERE (venue) / doi ONLY — NEVER the title, NEVER body
797	    prose. Returns '' when the record declares no identity, so the adjudication input stays
798	    byte-identical to the bare cited span (fail-safe: no fabricated grounding). See the module V4
799	    note for the full faithfulness-CRITICAL safety argument: an identity-only header can ground a

content.text: Continuing to read the rest of this large file.

content.content:
800	    WHO/WHEN/WHERE attribution atom but never a WHAT (numeric/mechanism/effect) atom."""
801	    parts: list[str] = []
802	    authors = _format_authors(record.get("authors"))
803	    if authors:
804	        parts.append(f"authors: {authors}")
805	    year = record.get("year")
806	    if year not in (None, "", []):
807	        parts.append(f"year: {str(year).strip()}")
808	    venue = record.get("journal")
809	    if isinstance(venue, str) and venue.strip():
810	        parts.append(f"venue: {venue.strip()}")
811	    doi = record.get("doi")
812	    if isinstance(doi, str) and doi.strip():
813	        parts.append(f"doi: {doi.strip()}")
814	    if not parts:
815	        return ""
816	    return f"[{_PROVENANCE_HEADER_LABEL} | " + " | ".join(parts) + "]"

819	def _attach_provenance_metadata(record: dict, row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> None:
820	    """Carry the source's declared IDENTITY (authors / year / venue / tier) from the raw row into the
821	    normalized record so the whole-basket adjudication input can build a provenance header.
822	
823	    ADDITIVE IDENTITY ONLY — never body text, never the title. `doi` is already extracted upstream by
824	    the existing regex/structured path (the header reads that same `record['doi']`). `tier` is carried
825	    for the corroboration WEIGHT surfaced in the audit_map, not for the header. The coverage functions
826	    (`_entity_canonical_match` / `_claim_covers_entity`) read only doi/pmid/url + text, so these extra
827	    keys can NEVER affect entity coverage. Gated by the caller under `PG_GATE_B_WHOLE_BASKET` so an OFF
828	    run keeps the normalized record byte-identical to the pre-V4 shape."""
829	    authors = row.get("authors")
830	    if isinstance(authors, (list, tuple)) and any(str(a).strip() for a in authors):
831	        record["authors"] = [str(a).strip() for a in authors if str(a).strip()]
832	    elif isinstance(authors, str) and authors.strip():
833	        record["authors"] = authors.strip()
834	    year = row.get("year")
835	    if year not in (None, "", []):
836	        record["year"] = year
837	    journal = row.get("journal")
838	    if isinstance(journal, str) and journal.strip():
839	        record["journal"] = journal.strip()
840	    tier = row.get("tier")
841	    if isinstance(tier, str) and tier.strip():
842	        record["tier"] = tier.strip()

845	def _cited_window_text(full_text: str, token: Any) -> str:
846	    """Return the cited BOUNDED-WINDOW slice of ``full_text`` for one provenance token.
847	
848	    When ``PG_GATE_B_CITED_SPAN=1`` the evidence text handed to Sentinel/Judge is sliced to a
849	    bounded window around the cited ``[start:end]`` range (±``PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES``, default
850	    400) — the SAME tolerance strict_verify's local-window rescue uses, so the two faithfulness
851	    layers share ONE windowing policy. BOUNDED (not exact-slice) is deliberate and three-fold safer:
852	    (1) it tolerates the 06-004-shape imprecise-but-real citation that strict_verify also tolerates;
853	    (2) ``token.start/end`` index the RAW ``direct_quote`` while the record text here was
854	    ``_row_text``-STRIPPED, so an exact slice would be offset-shifted by any leading whitespace —
855	    a bounded window absorbs that small shift; (3) fail-SAFE — a degenerate/empty window falls back
856	    to the whole text so the judge is never handed a blank span. Flag off (default) returns the whole
857	    text unchanged (byte-identical), so production is opt-in per the no-silent-downgrade rule; the
858	    re-run slate activates it.
859	    """
860	    if os.getenv(_GATE_B_CITED_SPAN_ENV, "0").strip() != "1":
861	        return full_text
862	    try:
863	        start = int(getattr(token, "start"))
864	        end = int(getattr(token, "end"))
865	    except (TypeError, ValueError, AttributeError):
866	        return full_text
867	    if end <= start:
868	        return full_text
869	    try:
870	        window = int(os.getenv(_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_ENV, str(_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_DEFAULT)))
871	    except ValueError:
872	        window = _GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_DEFAULT
873	    window = max(0, window)
874	    lo = max(0, start - window)
875	    hi = min(len(full_text), end + window)
876	    sliced = full_text[lo:hi].strip()
877	    return sliced or full_text
878

880	def _resolve_evidence(
881	    tokens: list[Any], evidence_lookup: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]]
882	) -> tuple[list[EvidenceDocument], list[Mapping[str, Any]]]:
883	    """Resolve each cited token -> (EvidenceDocument sliced to its cited window, source record).
884	
885	    FX-03 (#1107): one EvidenceDocument per TOKEN, carrying the bounded cited-window slice (see
886	    ``_cited_window_text``) instead of the whole record text, so Sentinel/Judge see the SPAN the
887	    decomposition prompt promises. Fail closed on an unknown id or empty record text.
888	    """
889	    documents: list[EvidenceDocument] = []
890	    records: list[Mapping[str, Any]] = []
891	    for token in tokens:
892	        evidence_id = token.evidence_id
893	        record = evidence_lookup.get(evidence_id)
894	        if record is None:
895	            raise ValueError(
896	                f"build_native_gate_b_inputs: unknown evidence_id {evidence_id!r}; every "
897	                f"cited token must resolve to an evidence record (fail-closed)."
898	            )
899	        text = str(record.get(_RECORD_TEXT_KEY) or "").strip()
900	        if not text:
901	            raise ValueError(
902	                f"build_native_gate_b_inputs: evidence_id {evidence_id!r} has empty "
903	                f"evidence text; cannot evaluate a claim against empty evidence (fail-closed)."
904	            )
905	        # FX-03 body window (UNCHANGED — the out-of-span/BUG-02 defense is preserved byte-for-byte).
906	        window_text = _normalize_span_text(_cited_window_text(text, token))
907	        # V4 (whole-basket, §-1.3): PREPEND the cited source's declared PROVENANCE IDENTITY header so
908	        # an attribution / identity atom grounds against the source's real authorship (recovering the
909	        # single-span false-negative), while the WHAT (factual/effect) atoms still judge against the
910	        # UNCHANGED body window. Empty header (record declares no identity) or flag OFF -> the text is
911	        # byte-identical to the bare span. See the module V4 note for the faithfulness-CRITICAL safety
912	        # argument (identity-only: never grounds a factual atom, shows the TRUE authors so a
913	        # misattribution still fails).
914	        if _whole_basket_enabled():
915	            header = _provenance_header(record)
916	            if header:
917	                window_text = f"{header}\n{window_text}"
918	        documents.append(EvidenceDocument(doc_id=evidence_id, text=window_text))
919	        records.append(record)
920	    return documents, records

923	def _section_is_gap_stub(section: Any) -> bool:
924	    """I-deepfix-001 (Codex grpC iter2 P1): True when a section is a rendered gap
925	    disclosure stub (the BB5-C07 / F10 / U24-withheld paths, and the fact-dedup
926	    re-resolve at multi_section_generator.py that sets is_gap_stub=True while leaving
927	    kept_sentences_pre_resolve populated). A gap stub renders ONLY the marker-less stub
928	    sentence — a non-claim — so it must contribute ZERO verified claims to the binding
929	    D8 four-role gate. Primary signal: the ``is_gap_stub`` flag (always set on gap stubs).
930	    Fallback for any section object predating the flag: a gap stub carries zero verified
931	    sentences AND an empty pre-resolve kept list, in which case it contributes nothing
932	    regardless. This SKIP only EXCLUDES withheld claims from the D8 INPUT; the frozen D8
933	    four-role verification logic is untouched.
934	    """
935	    if bool(getattr(section, "is_gap_stub", False)):
936	        return True
937	    kept = getattr(section, "kept_sentences_pre_resolve", None)
938	    verified = getattr(section, "sentences_verified", None)
939	    return verified == 0 and kept is not None and len(kept) == 0

942	def build_native_gate_b_inputs(
943	    *,
944	    multi: Any,
945	    template: dict,
946	    slug: str,
947	    domain: str,
948	    evidence_lookup: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]],
949	    model_slugs: dict[str, str],
950	    d8_config: D8PolicyConfig,
951	) -> NativeGateBBundle:
952	    """Build NATIVE 4-role Gate-B inputs from a finished report + native scope contract.
953	
954	    Pure function (no I/O, no network). Returns a `NativeGateBBundle` (inputs + audit_map);

967	        if s0_category is not None:

974	        # only the marker-less gap-disclosure stub (a non-claim); feeding its (withheld)
975	        # sentences into the binding D8 four-role gate would re-admit claims that never
976	        # reached the render — a faithfulness hole. Defense-in-depth alongside the

979	        # covers the downstream fact-dedup re-resolve path (which sets is_gap_stub=True while
980	        # leaving kept_sentences_pre_resolve populated). `continue` (not renumber) preserves

1188	                "evidence_ids": ds_evidence_ids,
1189	                "severity": _DEFAULT_OBSERVE_ONLY_SEVERITY,
1190	                "is_synthesized": True,
1191	                "tier": finding.get("tier"),

1194	            # of the claim set; the fail-closed depth reconcile (unjudged is_synthesized => drop)
1195	            # removes it from report.md so it never ships un-D8-gated.

1207	                )
1208	                continue
1209	            claims.append(
1210	                FourRoleClaim(
1211	                    claim_id=ds_claim_id,
1212	                    claim_text=audit_sentence,
1213	                    evidence_documents=ds_documents,
1214	                    severity=_DEFAULT_OBSERVE_ONLY_SEVERITY,
1215	                    s0_categories=[],
1216	                    covered_element_ids=[],
1217	                )
1218	            )
1219	
1220	    if not claims:
1221	        raise ValueError(
1222	            "build_native_gate_b_inputs: zero kept (verified) sentences; a 4-role "
1223	            "evaluation over no claims cannot produce a release decision (fail-closed, "
1224	            "no vacuous input)."
1225	        )
1226	
1227	    inputs = FourRoleEvaluationInputs(
1228	        claims=claims,
1229	        coverage_ledger=CoverageLedger(required_element_ids=required_element_ids),
1230	        required_s0_categories=sorted(required_s0_categories_set),
1231	        model_slugs=model_slugs,
1232	        rewrite_already_attempted=False,
1233	    )
