726	# === #1226 (I-pipe-001): blank-content bound + per-call wall-clock watchdog =====================
727	# THE FAILURE: a reasoning verifier (e.g. GLM-5.1 Mirror under xhigh) can burn its whole token
728	# budget on reasoning and emit EMPTY content every attempt; combined with the per-POST 900s timeout
729	# (_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) the COMPOSED retry loop (effort-ladder/provider-failover x transport-retries x
730	# rate-limit-backoff) can stall for HOURS per claim, so the D8 4-role gate never finishes and the
731	# whole sweep hangs. Two RELIABILITY backstops (NEVER touch a verify/NLI/4-role verdict; on
732	# exhaustion they re-raise the EXISTING fail-loud BlankVerdictError/RoleTransportError -> release
733	# stays HELD; no fake/empty verdict is ever synthesized):
734	#
735	#   (1) PG_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES (default 3): a HARD ceiling on the number of blank-content RETRIES.
736	#       Implemented by TRUNCATING the per-call effort/provider ladder to `1 + max_retries` attempts.
737	#       At the default (1 + 3 = 4) this ceiling is >= every role's existing default ladder length
738	#       (Judge 3, Mirror/routed-Sentinel 1+PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES=4, classifier-Sentinel 1,
739	#       decomposition-Sentinel 3), so the truncation is the IDENTITY at default — it only bites when
740	#       a custom PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES / PG_FOUR_ROLE_EFFORT_LADDER pushes a ladder past the cap.
741	#       Exhaustion fires the SAME existing `raise` at ladder-end -> the existing BlankVerdictError.
742	#
743	#   (2) PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S (default 3600s/1h): a COOPERATIVE wall-clock watchdog over the whole
744	#       `complete()` retry loop (checked at the top of each attempt + before each rate-limit sleep).
745	#       It does NOT interrupt an in-flight POST (httpx's _TIMEOUT_SECONDS bounds that) — it bounds the
746	#       COMPOSITION so a stuck-in-retries call aborts LOUDLY instead of hanging the sweep. 1h is a
747	#       deliberately-generous BACKSTOP against a wedged call, NOT a tight SLO: it sits well above any
748	#       HEALTHY 4-role call (a healthy claim resolves in seconds-to-minutes — the ladder rarely runs
749	#       to exhaustion, and a single blanking call returns its empty 200 quickly, it does not consume
750	#       the full per-POST 900s). The worst-case LEGITIMATE composition (every rung waiting near the
751	#       full _TIMEOUT_SECONDS, plus transport-retries and rate-limit backoff) CAN approach/exceed 1h;
752	#       that path is already pathological (it IS the stall this fix targets), so aborting it loudly is
753	#       the intended behavior, and the operator RAISES PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S if a slower legitimate
754	#       budget is ever needed (LAW VI). On trip it re-raises the last BlankVerdictError (if the loop
755	#       was blanking) else a RoleTransportError (both fail-loud; never a synthesized verdict).
756	#
757	# KILL-SWITCH (default-ON correct fix): PG_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG (default "1"). Set "0"/"false"/"no" to
758	# REVERT to the prior behavior — when OFF neither the ladder truncation nor the watchdog check ever
759	# fires, so control flow is byte-identical to before #1226. (Reliability-only kill-switch pattern.)
760	# Read LAZILY per-call (mirrors the adjacent PG_* knobs) so an override set after import is honored.
761	_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG_ENV = "PG_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG"
762	_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES_ENV = "PG_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES"
763	_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES_DEFAULT = "3"
764	_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_ENV = "PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S"
765	_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_DEFAULT = "3600.0"
766	
767	# #1290 landmine-3 (TRACK termination-ROTATE): on a total-deadline FORCE-CLOSE the retry rebuilds the
768	# SAME body and re-POSTs to the SAME slow host (live: 339 same-host 300s force-closes, 0 rotations),
769	# so every retry re-eats the wall and the 4-role gate grinds. When this is ON, a force-close ADDS the
770	# targeted (slow) provider to `body["provider"]["ignore"]` so the NEXT retry — bounded by
771	# PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES — re-POSTs to the next healthy provider in `order`. SAME role/model on
772	# every host -> faithfulness-NEUTRAL (only WHICH provider the retry targets changes; the verdict
773	# parsing, role adjudication, and span-grounding are untouched). Reuses the existing judge env name
774	# (`PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE`, parsed identically to credibility_judge_caller / entailment_judge) but
775	# DEFAULTS ON for the 4-role transport (the two judge consumers default it OFF; run_gate_b forces it
776	# ON for the benchmark anyway). The on-default is intentional: the operator wants the grind fixed by
777	# default on the paid 4-role path. LAW VI: env-gated, read lazily so a runtime override is honored.
778	_ENV_FORCE_CLOSE_PROVIDER_ROTATE = "PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE"
779	
780	# I-deepfix-001 B11 C2 (#1344): min-tok/s SLO slow-trickle EARLY rotate. A provider that returns a
781	# valid 200 but at a CRAWL (e.g. a trickling minimax Sentinel leg) does NOT trip the blank/timeout
782	# paths, so the run silently eats minutes/claim and the D8 seam grinds — the same trickle the
783	# total-deadline force-close was built for, but BELOW the deadline. When this is ON and a SUCCESSFUL
784	# call's observed throughput (completion_tokens / wall-seconds) is below PG_ROLE_MIN_TPS, AND a
785	# retry budget remains AND a rotation target exists, we add the slow provider to the ignore list and
786	# RE-ENTER the EXISTING force-close rotation (retry to the next healthy host) — exactly the path that
787	# flips a stuck `adjudicated False -> True`. FAIL-OPEN (the keystone safety rule): if no retry budget
788	# OR no other provider remains, we KEEP the slow-but-VALID verdict — a successful adjudication is
789	# NEVER discarded to chase speed (that would LOWER adjudication, the opposite of the goal).
790	# Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: same model, same verdict parsing; only WHICH provider serves the retry
791	# changes. LAW VI: env-driven (default 0.0 == OFF/no-op == byte-identical to pre-#1344), read lazily.
792	_ENV_ROLE_MIN_TPS = "PG_ROLE_MIN_TPS"
793	# Bound the SLO-triggered rotations per call so a chain where EVERY host trickles cannot loop the
794	# rotation indefinitely; once exhausted we keep the (slow) valid verdict (fail-open). Env-overridable.
795	_ENV_ROLE_MIN_TPS_MAX_ROTATIONS = "PG_ROLE_MIN_TPS_MAX_ROTATIONS"
796	_ROLE_MIN_TPS_MAX_ROTATIONS_DEFAULT = "2"

799	def role_min_tps() -> float:
800	    """I-deepfix-001 B11 C2: the min-tok/s SLO floor for a SUCCESSFUL role call (default 0.0 = OFF).
801	
802	    A POSITIVE value arms the slow-trickle early-rotate; <=0 / unparseable disables it (no-op). LAW
803	    VI: env-driven, read lazily so a runtime/slate override is honored."""
804	    raw = os.getenv(_ENV_ROLE_MIN_TPS, "0").strip()
805	    try:
806	        value = float(raw)
807	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
808	        return 0.0
809	    return value if value > 0 else 0.0

812	def role_min_tps_max_rotations() -> int:
813	    """I-deepfix-001 B11 C2: cap on SLO-triggered rotations per call (default 2). Clamped >= 0."""
814	    try:
815	        return max(0, int(os.getenv(_ENV_ROLE_MIN_TPS_MAX_ROTATIONS, _ROLE_MIN_TPS_MAX_ROTATIONS_DEFAULT)))
816	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
817	        return int(_ROLE_MIN_TPS_MAX_ROTATIONS_DEFAULT)

820	def _observed_tokens_per_second(usage: dict | None, elapsed_s: float) -> float | None:
821	    """Pure: completion tokens-per-second from a usage block + wall-seconds, else None.
822	
823	    Returns None (NOT a number) when throughput cannot be honestly computed — no usage, no
824	    completion_tokens, or a non-positive elapsed — so the caller treats it as "unknown" and does NOT
825	    rotate (fail-open: never penalize a call whose speed we cannot measure). Testable in isolation.
826	    """
827	    if not isinstance(usage, dict) or elapsed_s is None or elapsed_s <= 0:
828	        return None
829	    tokens = usage.get("completion_tokens")
830	    if tokens is None:
831	        details = usage.get("completion_tokens_details")
832	        if isinstance(details, dict):
833	            tokens = details.get("text_tokens")
834	    try:
835	        tok = int(tokens)
836	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
837	        return None
838	    if tok <= 0:
839	        return None
840	    return tok / elapsed_s
841

843	def role_min_tps_rotation_enabled() -> bool:
844	    """I-deepfix-001 B11 C2: armed iff a positive SLO floor AND the (shared) force-close rotation
845	    machinery are both enabled. The rotation REUSES the force-close ignore-list path, so it can only
846	    fire when force_close_provider_rotation_enabled() is also ON (its default). LAW VI."""
847	    return role_min_tps() > 0 and force_close_provider_rotation_enabled()

850	def force_close_provider_rotation_enabled() -> bool:
851	    """Whether a total-deadline force-close ADVANCES off the slow provider (default ON).
852	
853	    Parsed IDENTICALLY to the judge consumers' truthy set (`1/true/yes/on`) so a benchmark that sets
854	    `PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE=1` keeps it on here too; the ONLY difference is the default — ON here
855	    (vs OFF in credibility_judge_caller / entailment_judge), because the operator wants the
856	    force-close grind rotated by default on the 4-role transport. Any other value (`0/false/no/off`)
857	    reverts to the pre-rotation byte-identical behavior (retry the same provider).
858	    """
859	    return os.getenv(_ENV_FORCE_CLOSE_PROVIDER_ROTATE, "1").strip().lower() in (
860	        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
861	    )

864	def _targeted_provider_for_force_close(body: dict) -> str | None:
865	    """The provider a force-closed POST was (almost certainly) stuck on, for the rotate-ignore list.
866	
867	    A force-close has NO response body (unlike the blank-200 path, which reads `raw["provider"]`), so
868	    the slow host is inferred from the pinned routing: under `allow_fallbacks: False` OpenRouter
869	    attempts `provider.order` in rank sequence and (live evidence: 339 SAME-host 300s force-closes)
870	    sticks on the FIRST not-yet-ignored entry rather than cascading. So the targeted host is the first
871	    `order` slug not already in `ignore`. Returns None when routing is absent / order is empty / every
872	    listed provider is already ignored — in which case the caller no-ops the rotation and retries as
873	    before (forward progress is still guaranteed: a different head is targeted whenever one exists).
874	    """
875	    provider = body.get("provider")
876	    if not isinstance(provider, dict):
877	        return None
878	    order = provider.get("order")
879	    if not isinstance(order, list) or not order:
880	        return None
881	    ignore = provider.get("ignore")
882	    ignored = {str(s) for s in ignore} if isinstance(ignore, list) else set()
883	    for slug in order:
884	        slug_s = str(slug)
885	        if slug_s and slug_s not in ignored:
886	            return slug_s
887	    return None

890	def _nonignored_provider_remains(body: dict, *, also_ignore: str | None = None) -> bool:
891	    """True iff ``provider.order`` lists at least one slug NOT already in ``ignore`` (optionally also
892	    excluding ``also_ignore``). Used to FAIL-OPEN the SLO slow-rotate: if ignoring the slow host would
893	    leave NO provider to retry against, the valid (if slow) verdict MUST be KEPT — never discarded into
894	    a guaranteed no-provider failure. Mirrors ``_targeted_provider_for_force_close``'s order/ignore view.
895	    """
896	    provider = body.get("provider")
897	    if not isinstance(provider, dict):
898	        return False
899	    order = provider.get("order")
900	    if not isinstance(order, list) or not order:
901	        return False
902	    ignore = provider.get("ignore")
903	    ignored = {str(s) for s in ignore} if isinstance(ignore, list) else set()
904	    if also_ignore:
905	        ignored.add(str(also_ignore))
906	    return any(str(slug) and str(slug) not in ignored for slug in order)

954	# the `Retry-After` response header, is RESILIENCE ONLY: exhausted retries STILL raise
955	# RoleTransportError below -> release HELD (fail-closed; the gate is never weakened). LAW VI:
956	# every knob is env-driven (named, no magic numbers), read LAZILY per-call (mirrors the adjacent
957	# PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES pattern so an override set after import is honored).
958	#
959	# I-wire-003 B1 (#1317): the certify run hit 21 judge HTTP-429s and certified only 153/1220 claims
960	# in ~77 min — a SELF-INFLICTED 429 STORM (PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_WORKERS=12 x 3 roles -> up to ~36
961	# concurrent POSTs hammering the qwen judge) made WORSE by a WEAK flat-exponential backoff (no
962	# jitter -> every storming worker that 429s at the same instant retries in lock-step at the SAME
963	# delay, re-colliding). Two fixes here, plus a bounded JUDGE-CONCURRENCY semaphore below:
964	#   - MORE retries (5 -> 8): a steady-but-slower judge under a real rate limit needs more attempts
965	#     to ride out a sustained throttle window before it fails closed.
966	#   - FULL-JITTER exponential backoff (`_compute_backoff_delay`): de-correlates the retry herd so
967	#     storming workers no longer retry in lock-step. AWS-architecture-blog "Exponential Backoff And
968	#     Jitter" — full jitter (sleep = uniform(0, capped_exp)) minimizes total work + collision vs
969	#     equal/decorrelated jitter. A server-supplied `Retry-After` is honored AS-IS (clamped to cap),
970	#     NOT jittered (the server told us exactly when to come back).
971	_ROLE_HTTP_RETRY_MAX_DEFAULT = "8"
972	_ROLE_HTTP_RETRY_STATUS_DEFAULT = "429,503"

977	def _parse_retry_after_seconds(value: str | None) -> float | None:
978	    """Parse the `Retry-After` header as integer seconds (RFC 9110 delta-seconds form).
979	
980	    Returns the non-negative float seconds on success, or `None` when the header is absent,
981	    empty, or a non-numeric/HTTP-date form — in which case the caller falls back to the capped
982	    exponential backoff. We deliberately do NOT parse the HTTP-date form (per the I-beatboth-429
983	    spec): any non-integer value is treated as the absent case so a malformed header can never
984	    yield a negative or unbounded sleep.
985	    """
986	    if value is None:
987	        return None
988	    try:
989	        seconds = int(value.strip())
990	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
991	        return None
992	    return float(seconds) if seconds >= 0 else None

1000	    rng: random.Random | None = None,
1001	) -> float:
1002	    """The seconds to sleep before retry `attempt` (0-based) — FULL-JITTER backoff (I-wire-003 B1).
1003	
1004	    Pure + deterministic-under-injected-rng, so the schedule (grows, jitters, caps) is unit-testable
1005	    with NO live calls. Two cases:
1006	
1007	    1. A parsed, NON-negative server `Retry-After` (already integer-seconds via
1008	       `_parse_retry_after_seconds`) is honored AS-IS, only CLAMPED to `cap` (a hostile/misconfigured
1009	       7200s header can never make the judge sleep past the cap and stall the D8 run). NOT jittered:
1010	       the server stated exactly when to retry.
1011	    2. No usable header -> FULL JITTER over the capped exponential window: `ceiling = min(cap,
1012	       base * 2**attempt)`, returned delay = `uniform(0, ceiling)`. Full jitter (AWS architecture
1013	       blog, "Exponential Backoff And Jitter") de-correlates a retry herd: storming workers that all
1014	       429'd at the same instant no longer wake in lock-step and re-collide, which is what turns a
1015	       transient throttle into a self-sustaining 429 storm.
1016	
1017	    `rng` (default the module `random`) is injectable so a test can pin `random.uniform` and assert
1018	    the schedule mechanically. `base`/`cap` are clamped non-negative; the exponent is bounded so a
1019	    pathological `attempt` cannot overflow before the `min(cap, ...)` clamp.
1020	    """
1021	    base = max(0.0, base)
1022	    cap = max(0.0, cap)
1023	    if retry_after is not None:
1024	        return min(cap, max(0.0, retry_after))
1025	    # Bound the exponent BEFORE the shift so a huge `attempt` cannot produce a giant int (the
1026	    # min(cap, ...) clamps it anyway, but we never want to materialize an enormous intermediate).
1027	    safe_attempt = min(max(0, attempt), 30)
1028	    ceiling = min(cap, base * (2 ** safe_attempt))
1029	    if ceiling <= 0.0:
1030	        return 0.0
1031	    sampler = rng if rng is not None else random
1032	    return sampler.uniform(0.0, ceiling)

1041	# number is per-query, not cumulative across a multi-query process. Lock-guarded because the 4-role
1042	# seam increments it from up to PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_WORKERS concurrent worker threads.

1067	# === I-wire-003 B1 (#1317): bounded JUDGE concurrency throttle (lazy-init semaphore) ============
1068	# THE STORM SOURCE: PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_WORKERS concurrent claim workers each fire mirror->sentinel->
1069	# judge, so the qwen Judge (the single rate-limited model) sees up to `claim_workers` simultaneous
1070	# POSTs. A small STEADY concurrency UNDER the sustainable OpenRouter rate finishes FASTER than a
1071	# storm that 429s and burns minutes in backoff. This module-level semaphore caps how many Judge
1072	# POSTs run AT ONCE, regardless of how many claim workers exist; mirror/sentinel parallelism is
1073	# UNTOUCHED (only the judge role acquires it). FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: it changes only HOW MANY judge
1074	# calls run concurrently, never which claim passes/holds.
1075	#
1076	# Import-timing (the documented _CLAIM_WORKERS / set_four_role_reasoning_effort hazard): the size is
1077	# read LAZILY on FIRST acquire (not at import), so the benchmark slate's PG_FOUR_ROLE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY
1078	# set AFTER this module imports is honored — no rebind hook needed. The semaphore is acquired around
1079	# the WHOLE judge complete() call and released in `finally` (held across backoff sleeps too), so the
1080	# cap genuinely bounds concurrent load rather than letting a backing-off judge yield its slot.
1081	# LAW VI: env-tunable; default a conservative single-digit value, calibrated by the manifest counter.
1082	_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY_ENV = "PG_FOUR_ROLE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY"
1083	_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY_DEFAULT = "4"
1084	_JUDGE_SEMAPHORE_LOCK = threading.Lock()
1085	_JUDGE_SEMAPHORE: threading.BoundedSemaphore | None = None
1086	_JUDGE_SEMAPHORE_SIZE: int | None = None

1089	def judge_concurrency_limit() -> int:
1090	    """The bounded max concurrent Judge POSTs (PG_FOUR_ROLE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY, default 4; min 1).
1091	
1092	    Read lazily so a slate/env override set after import is honored. Clamped to >= 1 (a 0/negative
1093	    value would deadlock every judge call); an unparseable override falls back to the default.
1094	    """
1095	    raw = os.getenv(_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY_ENV, _JUDGE_CONCURRENCY_DEFAULT)
1096	    try:
1097	        value = int(raw)
1098	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
1099	        value = int(_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY_DEFAULT)
1100	    return max(1, value)

1103	def _judge_semaphore() -> threading.BoundedSemaphore:
1104	    """The process-wide Judge-concurrency semaphore, lazily sized from the env on first use.
1105	
1106	    Built once at first acquire (NOT at import) so the benchmark slate's value — set AFTER this
1107	    module imports, the documented _CLAIM_WORKERS import-timing hazard — is honored without a rebind
1108	    hook. If the env was raised between calls the semaphore is rebuilt at the new size; lowering
1109	    mid-run is intentionally NOT honored (a live BoundedSemaphore cannot shrink below outstanding
1110	    acquires without risking a ValueError on release), which is safe — the run only ever loosens.
1111	    """
1112	    global _JUDGE_SEMAPHORE, _JUDGE_SEMAPHORE_SIZE
1113	    desired = judge_concurrency_limit()
1114	    with _JUDGE_SEMAPHORE_LOCK:
1115	        if _JUDGE_SEMAPHORE is None or (_JUDGE_SEMAPHORE_SIZE or 0) < desired:
1116	            _JUDGE_SEMAPHORE = threading.BoundedSemaphore(desired)
1117	            _JUDGE_SEMAPHORE_SIZE = desired
1118	        return _JUDGE_SEMAPHORE

1315	def _build_openrouter_body(request: RoleRequest, model_slug: str, normalized_messages: list[dict]) -> dict:
1316	    """Assemble the OpenRouter request body: the SAME OpenAI-compatible body the self-host
1317	    transport builds, MINUS the top-level `documents` key (P1-4), PLUS the MAX-reasoning param.
1318	
1319	    Reuses `_build_body` for `model` + `messages` + the explicit passthrough allowlist
1320	    (structured_outputs / response_format / documents / max_tokens — POLARIS-internal keys like
1321	    pass2_input / citations / system never reach the body). Then (P1-4) DROPS the top-level
1322	    `documents` key, which OpenRouter's chat-completions schema does not define and which would
1323	    fail routing under `provider.require_parameters=True` — the evidence is already rendered into
1324	    `messages` by `_normalize_messages`, so model-visibility is preserved. Then sets
1325	    `reasoning = {"enabled": True, "effort": <_REASONING_EFFORT>}` — the openrouter_client
1326	    `reasoning_enabled` shape (openrouter_client.py:1424). Per OpenRouter docs the mutually
1327	    exclusive pair is `reasoning.effort` vs `reasoning.max_tokens`; the TOP-LEVEL `max_tokens` is
1328	    NOT exclusive with effort and MUST exceed the reasoning budget. Under `effort=xhigh` the
1329	    provider spends ~95% of top-level `max_tokens` on reasoning, so a popped/absent `max_tokens`
1330	    starves the verdict. I-meta-008 FULL-POWER therefore SETS a generous top-level `max_tokens`
1331	    (PG_D8_VERDICT_MAX_TOKENS, default 16384) for the reasoning Judge's verdict and an explicit
1332	    small classifier budget (PG_SENTINEL_MAX_TOKENS, default 256) for the non-reasoning Sentinel.
1333	    Source: https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/best-practices/reasoning-tokens
1334	    """
1335	    body = _build_body(request, model_slug, normalized_messages)
1336	    # P1-4: strip OpenRouter-unsupported top-level keys (currently `documents`). The evidence is
1337	    # already model-visible in `messages` (rendered by `_normalize_messages`), so the model still
1338	    # sees it; this only removes the schema-invalid body key that breaks require_parameters routing.
1339	    for key in _OPENROUTER_UNSUPPORTED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS:
1340	        body.pop(key, None)
1341	    # Codex iter-2 P1: reasoning is PER-ROLE. Only the deliberative verifiers (Mirror/Judge) get
1342	    # MAX reasoning + `provider.require_parameters=True`. The Sentinel classifier gets NEITHER —
1343	    # its OpenRouter slug does not advertise `reasoning`, so `require_parameters=True` would refuse
1344	    # to route and fail the first call. See `role_reasoning_enabled` for the full rationale.
1345	    if role_reasoning_enabled(request.role, request.model_slug):
1346	        # I-judge-kimi: the kimi Judge gets a BARE {"enabled": True} block (kimi advertises no
1347	        # supported_efforts, so any effort tier risks a 400); non-kimi reasoning slugs (Sentinel)
1348	        # keep {"enabled": True, "effort": ...}. Reasoning stays ON for every reasoning role.
1349	        body["reasoning"] = _judge_reasoning_block(model_slug, _REASONING_EFFORT)
1350	        # require_parameters=True makes OpenRouter ONLY route to a provider that actually HONORS the
1351	        # `reasoning` param (OpenRouter's default when absent is False — it could otherwise route a
1352	        # verifier to a provider that silently IGNORES reasoning, defeating the operator's
1353	        # MAX-reasoning purpose). Mirrors openrouter_client._call_impl's provider block
1354	        # (openrouter_client.py:1484-1487, OPENROUTER_REQUIRE_PARAMETERS default "true"). The
1355	        # I-bug-946 singleton-routing half (resolved per-role provider) is M4/PENDING and
1356	        # intentionally NOT here.
1357	        # I-run11-007 (#1051): pin to the ranked HEALTHY provider chain (order + ignore +
1358	        # allow_fallbacks:False) so a verifier call never lands on a slow/flaky provider.
1359	        body["provider"] = apply_provider_routing({"require_parameters": True}, request.role)
1360	        # I-meta-008 FULL-POWER (CORRECTS #1017): a reasoning verifier MUST carry a GENEROUS top-level
1361	        # `max_tokens`, NOT have it popped. OpenRouter `effort=xhigh` allocates ~95% of `max_tokens`
1362	        # to the reasoning budget, and `max_tokens` MUST be strictly higher than that budget so the
1363	        # bare verdict has room (docs: best-practices/reasoning-tokens). It is `reasoning.effort` vs
1364	        # `reasoning.max_tokens` that are mutually exclusive — NOT top-level `max_tokens`. The prior
1365	        # #1017 fix popped it, so xhigh ate ~95% of an unknown provider default and STARVED the verdict
1366	        # to empty for a reasoning-first Mirror (GLM-5.1) / Judge (Qwen) -> fail-loud crash on the run.
1367	        # 16384 -> reasoning ~15564 + verdict room ~820 (generous for Mirror JSON + Judge enum).
1368	        #

1370	        # {verdict, unsupported_atoms, atoms} body — a multi-atom list needs MORE output room than a
1371	        # bare verdict, AND the certification used reasoning + max_tokens>=3000. So the decomposition
1372	        # Sentinel gets its OWN generous budget (default 16384, hard-floored at 3000 so an env
1373	        # override can never re-introduce the run-12 truncation that collapses every claim to a
1374	        # fail-closed UNGROUNDED). The reasoning Judge gets a verdict-sized PG_D8_VERDICT_MAX_TOKENS (FIX #2).

1501	def _openrouter_served_block(raw: dict) -> dict:
1502	    """Stash the genuinely-OpenRouter-served identity under `_pathb_served` for the M4
1503	    served==pinned gate.
1504	
1505	    OpenRouter reports the served `provider` / `model` / `system_fingerprint` at the top level
1506	    of the response body (unlike a self-host vLLM, whose served identity is its `endpoint`). We
1507	    surface exactly those three, dropping any that are absent — `build_response_metadata`
1508	    consumes `provider`/`model`/`system_fingerprint` from `_pathb_served` and never substitutes
1509	    a request-derived value, so a response that fails to report its served identity fails the
1510	    gate loud rather than passing on request-derived data.
1511	    """
1512	    served: dict = {}
1513	    for key in ("provider", "model", "system_fingerprint"):
1514	        value = raw.get(key)
1515	        if value:
1516	            served[key] = value
1517	    return served

1520	# I-deepfix-001 B6 FIX 1 (#1370) — leaked-`<think>`-opener strip when a SEPARATE reasoning channel
1521	# exists. A subset of moonshotai/kimi-k2.6's 21 load-balanced OpenRouter endpoints populate
1522	# `message.reasoning` AND ALSO leak a literal `<think>` opener (sometimes with the verdict token
1523	# doubled, sometimes an unclosed block) into `message.content`, ignoring the response_format schema.
1524	# The pre-fix `_parse_openrouter_response` set `bare = content` VERBATIM whenever a separate reasoning
1525	# field was populated, so "<think>\nVERIFIED" flowed to the exact-enum `parse_judge_verdict` ->
1526	# JudgeEnumError -> a wasted paid re-ask. Default-ON LAW-VI kill-switch; OFF => byte-identical pre-fix.
1527	_THINK_LEAK_STRIP_ENV = "PG_OPENROUTER_THINK_LEAK_STRIP"
1528	
1529	
1530	def _think_leak_strip_enabled() -> bool:
1531	    """True iff the leaked-`<think>`-opener strip runs when a SEPARATE reasoning channel is present
1532	    (B6 FIX 1, default ON kill-switch). OFF => `bare = content` verbatim (byte-identical pre-fix).
1533	    Read at call time (LAW VI) so a slate/test override after import wins."""
1534	    return os.getenv(_THINK_LEAK_STRIP_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
1535

1537	def _strip_leaked_think_opener(content: object, reasoning: str) -> tuple[object, str]:
1538	    """Strip a leaked `<think>` opener off `content` when the reasoning already rode a SEPARATE field.
1539	
1540	    Returns `(bare, reasoning)`. Safe ONLY because a populated separate reasoning channel means the
1541	    leaked opener is provider CHROME (the chain-of-thought is not lost), NOT a half-emitted reasoning
1542	    block — so this never suppresses the fail-loud unclosed-`<think>` case, which only fires on the
1543	    both-fields-empty `_separate_reasoning` path (untouched):
1544	      - `content` not leading with `<think>` -> unchanged (byte-identical).
1545	      - a CLOSED leading `<think>...</think>` block -> split it off; the inner think text is APPENDED
1546	        to the separate reasoning (both channels preserved); `bare` = the stripped remainder.
1547	      - an UNCLOSED leading `<think>` (no `</think>`) -> strip ONLY the opener token + the immediately
1548	        following whitespace/newlines; `bare` = the remainder.
1549	    FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: the exact-enum `parse_judge_verdict` is untouched downstream —
1550	    'VERIFIEDVERIFIED' still raises `JudgeEnumError`; this only removes the leaked opener CHROME so a
1551	    genuine single enum token ('<think>\\nVERIFIED') parses on the first POST instead of re-asking."""
1552	    if not (isinstance(content, str) and content.lstrip().startswith(_THINK_OPEN)):
1553	        return content, reasoning
1554	    stripped = content.lstrip()
1555	    close_idx = stripped.find(_THINK_CLOSE)
1556	    if close_idx != -1:
1557	        inner = stripped[len(_THINK_OPEN):close_idx].strip()
1558	        bare = stripped[close_idx + len(_THINK_CLOSE):].strip()
1559	        if inner:
1560	            reasoning = f"{reasoning}\n{inner}" if reasoning else inner
1561	        return bare, reasoning
1562	    # No closing tag: strip ONLY the leading opener + immediately-following whitespace (case b).
1563	    return stripped[len(_THINK_OPEN):].lstrip(), reasoning
1564

1566	def _parse_openrouter_response(raw: dict) -> tuple[object, str | None, dict | None, str | None]:
1567	    """OpenRouter-aware `(raw_text, served_model, usage, reasoning)` extraction (P1-3).
1568	
1569	    Identical fail-loud contract to the self-host `_parse_response` (a missing `choices`, a
1570	    non-dict choice / message, or a blank bare verdict after reasoning separation raises
1571	    `RoleTransportError` — an empty/blank/reasoning-only completion is never a valid verifier
1572	    answer). The ONLY difference is the reasoning-extraction precedence: OpenRouter returns the
1573	    verifier's chain-of-thought in `message.reasoning` (NOT `reasoning_content`, NOT an inline
1574	    `<think>` block) per https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/best-practices/reasoning-tokens . So
1575	    the precedence here is: `reasoning_content` (vLLM reasoning-parser shape, kept for
1576	    cross-provider safety) -> `message.reasoning` (OpenRouter shape) -> a leading inline
1577	    `<think>` block split off `content`.
1578	
1579	    Reasoning stays SEPARATE from `raw_text` (I-meta-002-q1b): `raw_text` is ONLY the bare
1580	    verdict/body the verdict parsers consume; the reasoning rides on `RoleResponse.reasoning`.
1581	    """
1582	    choices = raw.get("choices")
1583	    if not choices:
1584	        # I-transport-001 (#1191) Site 3 (FIXES drb_75): a structurally-empty HTTP 200
1585	        # ({"choices": []}, model=None) previously raised a PLAIN RoleTransportError, which the
1586	        # complete() loop does NOT catch (it only catches BlankVerdictError at :904) -> release
1587	        # HELD at coverage 0.000 with NO provider failover (OpenRouter does not auto-advance off
1588	        # an empty 200). Raise the RECOVERABLE BlankVerdictError instead so the SAME effort-ladder
1589	        # + provider-exclusion failover that already handles a blank-content 200 (:902-973) excludes
1590	        # the blanking provider and advances to the next healthy one. This is scoped to the
1591	        # empty-choices case ONLY — the non-dict-choice / non-dict-message guards below KEEP their
1592	        # plain RoleTransportError (a malformed shape is a genuine fail-loud, not a provider blip).
1593	        # The gate stays fail-closed: HELD is reached only AFTER the ladder/providers exhaust
1594	        # (:973/:1008 raise); no fake verdict is ever synthesized.
1595	        raise BlankVerdictError(
1596	            f"OpenRouter response carried no choices (model={raw.get('model')!r})"
1597	        )
1598	    first_choice = choices[0]
1599	    if not isinstance(first_choice, dict):
1600	        raise RoleTransportError(
1601	            f"OpenRouter response choice was not an object (model={raw.get('model')!r})"
1602	        )
1603	    message = first_choice.get("message")
1604	    if not isinstance(message, dict):
1605	        raise RoleTransportError(
1606	            f"OpenRouter response choice carried no message object (model={raw.get('model')!r})"
1607	        )

1728	        # throttled roles (judge + sentinel): it probes UP after clean windows and HALVES on a 429/503
